--
Adds a new provider for [DeepSeek TUI](https://github.com/Hmbown/DeepSeek-TUI), a terminal-based AI coding as
sistant powered by DeepSeek V4 models.

## Changes

- New package: `pkg/providers/deepseek/` with 5 files (~500 lines)
- Registry: 1-line import + 2-line registration in `pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

## Provider details

**Session format**: JSON files in `~/.deepseek/sessions/<uuid>.json` with metadata (id, model, workspace, tim
estamps) and a messages array supporting text, thinking, tool_use, and tool_result content types.

**Implementation**:
- `Check()` — detects deepseek binary via `exec.LookPath` + `--version`
- `DetectAgent()` — matches sessions by workspace field in metadata
- `GetAgentChatSession/GetAgentChatSessions()` — JSON→schema.SessionData conversion with tool calls, thinking
 content, exchange grouping
- `ExecAgentAndWatch/WatchAgent()` — fsnotify watcher on `~/.deepseek/sessions/`
- `ListAgentChatSessions()` — lightweight metadata without full parsing
- System-injected `<turn_meta>` blocks are filtered from user messages

## Testing

Tested locally:
- `specstory check deepseek` — correctly detects binary and version
- `specstory sync deepseek` — converts sessions to markdown with thinking content in `<details>` blocks, tool
 calls rendered as `<tool-use>` blocks

Format your line by line review like this example:

```
  cmd/remote.go

  Line 12 (removed): Removed unused import of pkg/service package
  - (1) ✅ Good - Cleaning up unused imports is proper Go hygiene

  Lines 157-162 (modified): Changed from loading config directly to using RPC client
  // Old: config, err := service.LoadConfig(dir)
  // New: rpcClient, err := client.NewRPCClient(dir)
  - (2) ✅ Good architectural decision - Now operates via daemon RPC instead of direct file access

  ---
  pkg/remote/sync.go
  Lines 260-265 (modified): Success messages now come after RPC call
  - (10) ✅ Comment "config is now saved" is helpful context
  - (11) ⚠️ Misleading comment placement - comment says "config is now saved" but we can't be certain from this code's perspective (that happens in the daemon)

In addition to the line by line review, suggest the 2-5 most important improvements for this code review.

We focus our unit tests on complicated logic, and combinatorial scenarios, not on coverage or completeness for completeness sake. Better to not have a unit test than to have tests that are simplistic or tautological. We also don't test 3rd party library or language features, only our own code.

1	package deepseek

18	const (
19		defaultCommand = "deepseek"
20		versionFlag    = "--version"
21	)
22	
23	// Provider implements the spi.Provider interface for DeepSeek TUI.
24	type Provider struct{}
25	
26	// NewProvider creates a new DeepSeek TUI provider instance.
27	func NewProvider() *Provider {
28		return &Provider{}
29	}
30	
31	func (p *Provider) Name() string {
32		return "DeepSeek TUI"
33	}
34	
35	// Check verifies that DeepSeek TUI is available and returns its resolved
36	// location and version information.
37	func (p *Provider) Check(customCommand string) spi.CheckResult {
38		cmdName, _ := parseCommand(customCommand)
39		isCustom := strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != ""
40		slog.Info("Check: verifying DeepSeek TUI installation",
41			"command", cmdName, "customCommand", isCustom)
42	
43		resolved, err := exec.LookPath(cmdName)
44		if err != nil {
45			slog.Info("Check: binary not found on PATH", "command", cmdName, "error", err)
46			msg := buildCheckErrorMessage("not_found", cmdName, isCustom, "")
47			trackCheckFailure("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, "", versionFlag, "", "not_found", err.Error())
48			return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: "", ErrorMessage: msg}
49		}
50		slog.Info("Check: binary resolved", "command", cmdName, "resolved", resolved)
51	
52		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
53		cmd := exec.Command(resolved, versionFlag)
54		cmd.Stdout = &stdout
55		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
56		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
57			errorType := classifyCheckError(err)
58			stderrOutput := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
59			slog.Info("Check: version probe failed",
60				"resolved", resolved, "errorType", errorType, "stderr", stderrOutput, "error", err)
61			msg := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, resolved, isCustom, stderrOutput)
62			trackCheckFailure("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, versionFlag, stderrOutput, errorType, err.Error())
63			return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: resolved, ErrorMessage: msg}
64		}
65	
66		version := strings.TrimSpace(stdout.String())
67		if version == "" {
68			version = "unknown"
69		}
70		slog.Info("Check: succeeded", "resolved", resolved, "version", version)
71		trackCheckSuccess("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, version, versionFlag)
72		return spi.CheckResult{Success: true, Version: version, Location: resolved}
73	}
74	
75	// DetectAgent reports whether DeepSeek TUI has been used in the given project
76	// based on existing session files in ~/.deepseek/sessions/.
77	func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {
78		files, err := listSessionFiles()
79		if err != nil {
80			if helpOutput {
81				log.UserWarn("DeepSeek TUI detection failed: %v", err)
82			}
83			return false
84		}
85		if len(files) == 0 {
86			if helpOutput {
87				printDetectionHelp()
88			}
89			return false
90		}
91		if projectPath == "" {
92			return true
93		}
94		for _, file := range files {
95			if sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
96				return true
97			}
98		}
99		if helpOutput {
100			printDetectionHelp()
101		}
102		return false
103	}
104	
105	// GetAgentChatSessions returns all available agent chat sessions from DeepSeek TUI,
106	// optionally filtered to sessions associated with the given projectPath.
107	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
108		files, err := listSessionFiles()
109		if err != nil {
110			return nil, err
111		}
112	
113		normalizedProject := strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
114	
115		var matchingFiles []sessionFile
116		for _, file := range files {
117			if normalizedProject != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, normalizedProject) {
118				continue
119			}
120			matchingFiles = append(matchingFiles, file)
121		}
122	
123		totalFiles := len(matchingFiles)
124		result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(matchingFiles))
125	
126		for i, file := range matchingFiles {
127			session, err := parseSessionFile(file.Path, true)
128			if err != nil {
129				slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
130				if progress != nil {
131					progress(i+1, totalFiles)
132				}
133				continue
134			}
135			chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
136			if chat != nil {
137				result = append(result, *chat)
138			}
139			if progress != nil {
140				progress(i+1, totalFiles)
141			}
142		}
143		return result, nil
144	}
145	
146	// GetAgentChatSession returns the agent chat session with the given ID.
147	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
148		path, err := findSessionFileByID(sessionID)
149		if err != nil || path == "" {
150			return nil, err
151		}
152		if strings.TrimSpace(projectPath) != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(path, projectPath) {
153			return nil, nil
154		}
155		session, err := parseSessionFile(path, true)
156		if err != nil {
157			return nil, err
158		}
159		return convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw), nil
160	}
161	
162	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes DeepSeek TUI for the given project and watches
163	// for session updates.
164	func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
165		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: starting DeepSeek TUI execution and monitoring",
166			"projectPath", projectPath,
167			"customCommand", customCommand,
168			"resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID,
169			"debugRaw", debugRaw,
170			"hasCallback", sessionCallback != nil)
171	
172		if sessionCallback == nil {
173			slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: no callback provided, running without watcher")
174			return ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
175		}
176	
177		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
178		defer cancel()
179	
180		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: launching session watcher in background")
181		watchErr := make(chan error, 1)
182		go func() {
183			watchErr <- watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
184		}()
185	
186		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: executing DeepSeek TUI", "command", customCommand)
187		err := ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
188		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: DeepSeek TUI exited, stopping watcher", "execError", err)
189		cancel()
190	
191		if werr := <-watchErr; werr != nil && !errors.Is(werr, context.Canceled) {
192			slog.Warn("ExecAgentAndWatch: watcher stopped with error", "error", werr)
193		}
194	
195		if err != nil {
196			return fmt.Errorf("deepseek execution failed: %w", err)
197		}
198		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: complete")
199		return nil
200	}
201	
202	// WatchAgent monitors DeepSeek TUI sessions for the given project and invokes
203	// sessionCallback for each new or updated session until ctx is canceled.
204	func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
205		slog.Info("WatchAgent: starting DeepSeek TUI activity monitoring",
206			"projectPath", projectPath, "debugRaw", debugRaw)
207		if sessionCallback == nil {
208			return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
209		}
210		err := watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
211		slog.Info("WatchAgent: watcher exited", "error", err)
212		return err
213	}
214	
215	// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight session metadata without full parsing.
216	func (p *Provider) ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
217		files, err := listSessionFiles()
218		if err != nil {
219			return nil, err
220		}
221	
222		normalizedProject := strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
223	
224		var result []spi.SessionMetadata
225		for _, file := range files {
226			if normalizedProject != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, normalizedProject) {
227				continue
228			}
229	
230			metadata, err := extractSessionMetadata(file.Path)
231			if err != nil {
232				slog.Debug("deepseek: failed to extract session metadata", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
233				continue
234			}
235			if metadata == nil {
236				slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping empty session", "path", file.Path)
237				continue
238			}
239			result = append(result, *metadata)
240		}
241	
242		return result, nil
243	}
244	
245	// --- helpers shared across the package ---
246	
247	func classifyCheckError(err error) string {
248		var execErr *exec.Error
249		var pathErr *os.PathError
250		switch {
251		case errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound:
252			return "not_found"
253		case errors.As(err, &pathErr) && errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission):
254			return "permission_denied"
255		case errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission):
256			return "permission_denied"
257		default:
258			return "version_failed"
259		}
260	}
261	
262	func buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType string, command string, isCustom bool, stderr string) string {
263		var builder strings.Builder
264		switch errorType {
265		case "not_found":
266			builder.WriteString("DeepSeek TUI was not found.\n\n")
267			if isCustom {
268				builder.WriteString("• Verify the custom path you provided is executable.\n")
269				fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "• Provided command: %s\n", command)
270			} else {
271				builder.WriteString("• Install DeepSeek TUI and ensure `deepseek` is on your PATH.\n")
272				builder.WriteString("• Re-run `specstory check deepseek` after installation.\n")
273			}
274		case "permission_denied":
275			builder.WriteString("SpecStory cannot execute DeepSeek TUI due to permissions.\n\n")
276			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Try: chmod +x %s\n", command)
277		default:
278			builder.WriteString("`deepseek --version` failed.\n\n")
279			if stderr != "" {
280				builder.WriteString("Error output:\n")
281				builder.WriteString(stderr)
282				builder.WriteString("\n\n")
283			}
284			builder.WriteString("Run `deepseek --version` manually to diagnose, then retry.")
285		}
286		return builder.String()
287	}
288	
289	func trackCheckSuccess(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, version string, versionFlag string) {
290		props := analytics.Properties{
291			"provider":       provider,
292			"custom_command": custom,
293			"command_path":   commandPath,
294			"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
295			"version":        version,
296			"version_flag":   versionFlag,
297		}
298		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, props)
299	}
300	
301	func trackCheckFailure(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, versionFlag string, stderrOutput string, errorType string, message string) {
302		props := analytics.Properties{
303			"provider":       provider,
304			"custom_command": custom,
305			"command_path":   commandPath,
306			"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
307			"version_flag":   versionFlag,
308			"error_type":     errorType,
309			"error_message":  message,
310		}
311		if stderrOutput != "" {
312			props["stderr"] = stderrOutput
313		}
314		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, props)
315	}
316	
317	func printDetectionHelp() {
318		log.UserMessage("No DeepSeek TUI sessions found under ~/.deepseek/sessions yet.\n")
319		log.UserMessage("Run DeepSeek TUI inside this project to create a session, then rerun `specstory sync deepseek`.\n")
320	}

26	// dsSession represents the on-disk format of a DeepSeek TUI session file.
27	type dsSession struct {

29		Metadata      dsMetadata  `json:"metadata"`
30		Messages      []dsMessage `json:"messages"`
31		SystemPrompt  string      `json:"system_prompt"`
32		RawData       string      `json:"-"` // populated only when parseSessionFile is called with wantRawData=true
33	}

35	type dsMetadata struct {

37		Title        string `json:"title"`
38		CreatedAt    string `json:"created_at"`
39		UpdatedAt    string `json:"updated_at"`
40		MessageCount int    `json:"message_count"`
41		TotalTokens  int    `json:"total_tokens"`
42		Model        string `json:"model"`
43		Workspace    string `json:"workspace"`
44		Mode         string `json:"mode"`
45	}

49		Content []dsContentPart `json:"content"`
50	}
51	
52	type dsContentPart struct {
53		Type     string `json:"type"`     // "text", "thinking", "tool_use", "tool_result"
54		Text     string `json:"text"`     // used when Type == "text"
55		Thinking string `json:"thinking"` // used when Type == "thinking"
56		// tool_use fields are flat in the JSON (not nested)
57		Name  string         `json:"name"`
58		ID    string         `json:"id"`
59		Input map[string]any `json:"input"`
60		// tool_result fields
61		ToolResultContent string `json:"content"`     // tool_result content (raw string)
62		ToolUseID         string `json:"tool_use_id"` // matches the tool_use.id this result is for
63	}

65	// parseSessionFile reads and parses a DeepSeek TUI session JSON file. When
66	// wantRawData is true the file's full byte body is retained on the returned
67	// session for downstream cloud sync and debug-raw output; callers that only
68	// need the parsed structure (e.g. metadata extraction) pass false to avoid
69	// keeping a copy of the entire JSON in memory.
70	func parseSessionFile(path string, wantRawData bool) (*dsSession, error) {
71		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot read session file %s: %w", path, err)
74		}
75	
76		var session dsSession
77		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &session); err != nil {
78			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot parse session file %s: %w", path, err)
79		}
80	
81		if wantRawData {
82			session.RawData = string(data)
83		}
84		return &session, nil
85	}
86	
87	// extractSessionMetadata parses a session file and returns a SessionMetadata
88	// summary derived from the first user message. Returns (nil, nil) when the
89	// session has no usable user content — callers treat that as "skip this file".
90	func extractSessionMetadata(path string) (*spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
91		var result *spi.SessionMetadata
92	
93		session, err := parseSessionFile(path, false)
94		if err != nil {
95			return nil, err
96		}
97	
98		firstUserMsg := firstUserMessageText(session)
99		if firstUserMsg != "" {
100			slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMsg)
101			if slug == "" {
102				slug = "deepseek-session"
103			}
104			result = &spi.SessionMetadata{
105				SessionID: session.Metadata.ID,
106				CreatedAt: session.Metadata.CreatedAt,
107				Slug:      slug,
108				Name:      spi.GenerateReadableName(firstUserMsg),
109			}
110		}
111	
112		return result, nil
113	}
114	
115	// firstUserMessageText returns the text of the first user message that carries
116	// visible content, or "" if no such message exists.
117	func firstUserMessageText(session *dsSession) string {
118		for _, msg := range session.Messages {
119			if msg.Role != "user" {
120				continue
121			}
122			if text := msgContentText(msg); text != "" {
123				return text
124			}
125		}
126		return ""
127	}

129	// convertToAgentSession converts a parsed dsSession to the unified AgentChatSession format.
130	func convertToAgentSession(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
131		if session == nil {
132			return nil
133		}
134		if len(session.Messages) == 0 {
135			return nil
136		}
137	
138		sessionData, err := generateAgentSession(session, workspaceRoot)
139		if err != nil {
140			slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session due to conversion error",
141				"sessionId", session.Metadata.ID, "error", err)
142			return nil
143		}
144	
145		// Derive slug from first user message
146		slug := deriveSlug(session)
147		if slug == "" {
148			slug = "deepseek-session"
149		}
150	
151		if debugRaw {
152			writeDebugRaw(session)
153		}
154	
155		return &spi.AgentChatSession{
156			SessionID:   session.Metadata.ID,
157			CreatedAt:   sessionData.CreatedAt,
158			Slug:        slug,
159			SessionData: sessionData,
160			RawData:     session.RawData,
161		}
162	}
163	
164	// generateAgentSession converts a dsSession into the shared SessionData schema.
165	func generateAgentSession(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string) (*SessionData, error) {
166		workspace := strings.TrimSpace(workspaceRoot)
167		if ws := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.Workspace); ws != "" {
168			workspace = ws
169		}
170	
171		exchanges := buildExchanges(session, workspace)
172		if len(exchanges) == 0 {
173			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session contains no conversational exchanges")
174		}
175	
176		created := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.CreatedAt)
177		updated := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.UpdatedAt)
178		if updated == "" {
179			updated = created
180		}
181	
182		// DeepSeek TUI reports only a session-level total_tokens with no breakdown
183		// between input/output/cached, so we intentionally don't synthesize a Usage
184		// value — attributing the sum to InputTokens would mislead downstream stats.
185	
186		data := &SessionData{
187			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
188			Provider: ProviderInfo{
189				ID:      "deepseek-tui",
190				Name:    "DeepSeek TUI",
191				Version: session.Metadata.Model,
192			},
193			SessionID:     session.Metadata.ID,
194			CreatedAt:     created,
195			UpdatedAt:     updated,
196			Slug:          deriveSlug(session),
197			WorkspaceRoot: workspace,
198			Exchanges:     exchanges,
199		}
200	
201		return data, nil
202	}
203	
204	// buildExchanges groups messages into exchanges.
205	// Each user message with real text content starts a new exchange.
206	// User messages that only contain tool_result content are treated as
207	// part of the current assistant turn (tool response), not as new exchanges.
208	func buildExchanges(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string) []Exchange {
209		var exchanges []Exchange
210		var current *Exchange
211	
212		// DeepSeek TUI does not stamp per-message timestamps. Use session-level
213		// CreatedAt for user turns (best signal of when the conversation began)
214		// and UpdatedAt for assistant turns so re-parsing the same file produces
215		// stable, deterministic output. time.Now() would change every parse.
216		userTs := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.CreatedAt)
217		assistantTs := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.UpdatedAt)
218		if assistantTs == "" {
219			assistantTs = userTs
220		}
221	
222		for _, msg := range session.Messages {
223			if msg.Role == "user" {
224				if isToolResultOnly(&msg) {
225					// Tool results live in user-role messages; route each tool_result
226					// to the assistant Tool message it belongs to via tool_use_id.
227					if current != nil {
228						attachToolResults(&msg, current)
229					}
230					continue
231				}
232	
233				if current != nil && len(current.Messages) > 0 {
234					exchanges = append(exchanges, *current)
235				}
236				current = &Exchange{}
237	
238				spiMsg := convertUserMessage(msg, userTs)
239				if len(spiMsg.Content) > 0 {
240					current.Messages = append(current.Messages, spiMsg)
241					if current.StartTime == "" {
242						current.StartTime = userTs
243					}
244					current.EndTime = userTs
245				}
246				continue
247			}
248	
249			if current == nil {
250				current = &Exchange{}
251			}
252	
253			spiMsgs := convertAssistantMessage(msg, session.Metadata.Model, assistantTs, workspaceRoot)
254			current.Messages = append(current.Messages, spiMsgs...)
255			current.EndTime = assistantTs
256		}
257	
258		if current != nil && len(current.Messages) > 0 {
259			exchanges = append(exchanges, *current)
260		}
261	
262		for i := range exchanges {
263			exchanges[i].ExchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", session.Metadata.ID, i)
264			if exchanges[i].StartTime == "" {
265				exchanges[i].StartTime = session.Metadata.CreatedAt
266			}
267			if exchanges[i].EndTime == "" {
268				exchanges[i].EndTime = exchanges[i].StartTime
269			}
270		}
271	
272		return exchanges
273	}
274	
275	// isToolResultOnly returns true if the user message only contains tool_result
276	// content parts (no real text from the user).
277	func isToolResultOnly(msg *dsMessage) bool {
278		hasToolResult := false
279		for _, cp := range msg.Content {
280			switch cp.Type {
281			case "tool_result":
282				hasToolResult = true
283			case "text":
284				// Check if the text content is just <turn_meta> or system noise.
285				t := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
286				if t != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(t, "<turn_meta>") {
287					return false // has real user text
288				}
289			}
290		}
291		return hasToolResult
292	}
293	
294	// attachToolResults routes each tool_result content part in a user-role message
295	// to the assistant Tool message it belongs to, matched by tool_use_id.
296	//
297	// DeepSeek TUI emits tool results in the user message that follows the
298	// assistant's tool calls. When the assistant fires N parallel tool calls, the
299	// next user message holds N tool_result parts — each with its own tool_use_id.
300	// Earlier code attached every result to whichever was the *last* tool message
301	// and string-concatenated multiple results together; that produced wrong
302	// markdown for any session with parallel calls. We fix this by indexing
303	// assistant tool messages by UseID and attaching each result to its match.
304	//
305	// After attaching, we re-render FormattedMarkdown so the rendered tool block
306	// includes the result body.
307	func attachToolResults(msg *dsMessage, current *Exchange) {
308		if current == nil {
309			return
310		}
311		// Build an index of assistant Tool messages in this exchange by UseID.
312		byUseID := make(map[string]*Message, len(current.Messages))
313		for i := range current.Messages {
314			m := &current.Messages[i]
315			if m.Tool != nil && m.Tool.UseID != "" {
316				byUseID[m.Tool.UseID] = m
317			}
318		}
319	
320		for _, cp := range msg.Content {
321			if cp.Type != "tool_result" {
322				continue
323			}
324			target, ok := byUseID[cp.ToolUseID]
325			if !ok || target == nil || target.Tool == nil {
326				// Result with no matching assistant call — drop rather than misattribute.
327				slog.Debug("deepseek: tool_result has no matching tool_use",
328					"toolUseId", cp.ToolUseID)
329				continue
330			}
331			content := strings.TrimSpace(cp.ToolResultContent)
332			if content == "" {
333				continue
334			}
335			target.Tool.Output = map[string]any{"content": content}
336			// Re-render the formatted markdown now that the result is in place.
337			formatted := formatToolCall(target.Tool)
338			if formatted != "" {
339				target.Tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formatted
340			}
341		}
342	}
343	
344	// convertUserMessage converts a DeepSeek user message to the schema Message format.
345	// System-injected metadata blocks like <turn_meta> are filtered out so only the
346	// user's actual question appears in the saved session.
347	func convertUserMessage(msg dsMessage, timestamp string) Message {
348		parts := userContentParts(msg)
349		return Message{
350			Timestamp: timestamp,
351			Role:      "user",
352			Content:   parts,
353		}
354	}
355	
356	// userContentParts returns the user-visible text content parts from a message,
357	// skipping system-injected blocks such as <turn_meta>.
358	func userContentParts(msg dsMessage) []ContentPart {
359		var parts []ContentPart
360		for _, cp := range msg.Content {
361			if cp.Type != "text" || cp.Text == "" {
362				continue
363			}
364			if strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text), "<turn_meta>") {
365				continue
366			}
367			parts = append(parts, ContentPart{Type: "text", Text: cp.Text})
368		}
369		return parts
370	}
371	
372	// convertAssistantMessage converts a DeepSeek assistant message to the schema Message format.
373	// Each tool_use content part produces a separate Message (one tool = one message),
374	// while text and thinking parts are combined into a single message.
375	func convertAssistantMessage(msg dsMessage, model string, timestamp string, workspaceRoot string) []Message {
376		var msgs []Message
377		var textParts []ContentPart
378		var toolParts []dsContentPart
379	
380		for _, cp := range msg.Content {
381			switch cp.Type {
382			case "tool_use":
383				toolParts = append(toolParts, cp)
384			case "thinking":
385				thinking := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Thinking)
386				if thinking == "" {
387					thinking = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
388				}
389				if thinking != "" {
390					textParts = append(textParts, ContentPart{Type: "thinking", Text: thinking})
391				}
392			case "text":
393				text := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
394				if text == "" {
395					text = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Thinking)
396				}
397				if text != "" {
398					textParts = append(textParts, ContentPart{Type: "text", Text: text})
399				}
400			}
401		}
402	
403		if len(textParts) > 0 {
404			msgs = append(msgs, Message{
405				Timestamp: timestamp,
406				Role:      "agent",
407				Model:     model,
408				Content:   textParts,
409			})
410		}
411	
412		for _, cp := range toolParts {
413			msgs = append(msgs, convertToolUseMessage(cp, model, timestamp, workspaceRoot))
414		}
415	
416		return msgs
417	}
418	
419	// convertToolUseMessage creates a Message for a tool_use content part. The
420	// FormattedMarkdown is set from the tool's input only here; attachToolResults
421	// re-renders it once the matching tool_result is attached.
422	func convertToolUseMessage(cp dsContentPart, model string, timestamp string, workspaceRoot string) Message {
423		name := cp.Name
424		if name == "" {
425			name = "unknown"
426		}
427		tool := &ToolInfo{
428			Name:  name,
429			Type:  classifyToolType(name),
430			UseID: cp.ID,
431			Input: cp.Input,
432		}
433		if formatted := formatToolCall(tool); formatted != "" {
434			tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formatted
435		}
436		return Message{
437			Timestamp: timestamp,
438			Role:      "agent",
439			Model:     model,
440			Tool:      tool,
441			PathHints: extractPathHints(cp.Input, workspaceRoot),
442		}
443	}
444	
445	// classifyToolType maps a tool name to a SpecStory tool type.
446	func classifyToolType(name string) string {
447		writeTools := map[string]bool{
448			"write_file": true, "edit_file": true, "apply_patch": true,
449		}
450		readTools := map[string]bool{
451			"read_file": true, "list_dir": true, "retrieve_tool_result": true,
452		}
453		searchTools := map[string]bool{
454			"grep_files": true, "file_search": true, "web_search": true,
455			"fetch_url": true,
456		}
457		shellTools := map[string]bool{
458			"exec_shell": true, "task_shell_start": true, "task_shell_wait": true,
459			"exec_shell_wait": true, "exec_interact": true, "exec_shell_interact": true,
460		}
461		taskTools := map[string]bool{
462			"update_plan": true, "todo_write": true, "checklist_write": true,
463			"task_create": true, "task_read": true, "task_list": true,
464		}
465	
466		if writeTools[name] {
467			return schema.ToolTypeWrite
468		}
469		if readTools[name] {
470			return schema.ToolTypeRead
471		}
472		if searchTools[name] {
473			return schema.ToolTypeSearch
474		}
475		if shellTools[name] {
476			return schema.ToolTypeShell
477		}
478		if taskTools[name] {
479			return schema.ToolTypeTask
480		}
481	
482		return schema.ToolTypeGeneric
483	}
484	
485	// msgContentText extracts the first user-visible text content from a dsMessage,
486	// skipping system-injected blocks such as <turn_meta>.
487	func msgContentText(msg dsMessage) string {
488		for _, cp := range msg.Content {
489			if cp.Type == "text" && cp.Text != "" {
490				if strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text), "<turn_meta>") {
491					continue
492				}
493				return cp.Text
494			}
495		}
496		// Fallback: try thinking content or first non-empty.
497		for _, cp := range msg.Content {
498			t := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
499			if t == "" {
500				t = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Thinking)
501			}
502			if t != "" {
503				return t
504			}
505		}
506		return ""
507	}
508	
509	// deriveSlug creates a filename-safe slug from the first user message.
510	func deriveSlug(session *dsSession) string {
511		for _, msg := range session.Messages {
512			if msg.Role == "user" {
513				text := msgContentText(msg)
514				if text != "" {
515					return spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(text)
516				}
517			}
518		}
519		return "deepseek-session"
520	}
521	
522	// writeDebugRaw writes the raw session JSON to the debug directory.
523	func writeDebugRaw(session *dsSession) {
524		if session == nil {
525			return
526		}
527	
528		dir := spi.GetDebugDir(session.Metadata.ID)
529		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
530			slog.Debug("deepseek: unable to create debug dir", "error", err)
531			return
532		}
533	
534		rawPath := filepath.Join(dir, "raw-session.json")
535		if err := os.WriteFile(rawPath, []byte(session.RawData), 0o644); err != nil {
536			slog.Debug("deepseek: failed to write debug raw file", "path", rawPath, "error", err)
537			return
538		}
539	
540		slog.Debug("deepseek: wrote debug raw file", "sessionId", session.Metadata.ID, "path", rawPath)
541	}
542

 `./pkg/providers/deepseek/deepseek_exec.go`

```text
1	package deepseek
2	
3	import (
4		"log/slog"
5		"os"
6		"os/exec"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
11	)
12	
13	// expandTilde expands a leading ~ to the user's home directory so users can
14	// configure custom commands like "~/bin/deepseek".
15	func expandTilde(path string) string {
16		if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
17			return path
18		}
19		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
20		if err != nil {
21			return path
22		}
23		return filepath.Join(home, path[2:])
24	}
25	
26	// parseCommand splits a custom command string into executable name and args.
27	// Returns default command if customCommand is empty.
28	func parseCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {
29		if strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != "" {
30			parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
31			if len(parts) > 0 {
32				return expandTilde(parts[0]), parts[1:]
33			}
34		}
35		return defaultCommand, nil
36	}
37	
38	// ExecuteDeepSeek launches the DeepSeek TUI process and blocks until it exits.
39	func ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand string, resumeSessionID string) error {
40		cmdName, args := parseCommand(customCommand)
41		args = ensureResumeArgs(args, resumeSessionID)
42		if resumeSessionID != "" {
43			slog.Info("ExecuteDeepSeek: resuming session", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
44		}
45	
46		command := exec.Command(cmdName, args...)
47		command.Stdin = os.Stdin
48		command.Stdout = os.Stdout
49		command.Stderr = os.Stderr
50	
51		return command.Run()
52	}
53	
54	// ensureResumeArgs adds --resume <sessionID> to args if not already present.
55	// DeepSeek TUI uses --resume to continue an existing session.
56	func ensureResumeArgs(args []string, resumeSessionID string) []string {
57		if resumeSessionID == "" {
58			return args
59		}
60	
61		for i, arg := range args {
62			if arg == "--resume" || arg == "-r" {
63				if i+1 < len(args) && strings.TrimSpace(args[i+1]) != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(args[i+1], "-") {
64					return args
65				}
66				repaired := append([]string{}, args[:i+1]...)
67				repaired = append(repaired, resumeSessionID)
68				repaired = append(repaired, args[i+1:]...)
69				return repaired
70			}
71			if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--resume=") {
72				if strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(arg, "--resume=")) != "" {
73					return args
74				}
75				repaired := append([]string{}, args...)
76				repaired[i] = "--resume=" + resumeSessionID
77				return repaired
78			}
79		}
80	
81		return append(args, "--resume", resumeSessionID)
82	}
83
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./pkg/providers/deepseek/markdown_tools.go`

```text
1	package deepseek
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"sort"
8		"strconv"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
12	)
13	
14	// formatToolCall renders a ToolInfo into a markdown body that the session
15	// renderer wraps in a <tool-use><details> block. The body combines a tool's
16	// input rendering with its (optional) result. DeepSeek attaches results in a
17	// later user message, so this function is called twice: once when the tool_use
18	// is converted (input only), then again from attachToolResults once the
19	// matching tool_result is in place.
20	func formatToolCall(tool *ToolInfo) string {
21		if tool == nil {
22			return ""
23		}
24		body := strings.TrimSpace(formatToolInput(tool))
25		result := strings.TrimSpace(formatToolOutput(tool))
26	
27		var parts []string
28		if body != "" {
29			parts = append(parts, body)
30		}
31		if result != "" {
32			parts = append(parts, result)
33		}
34		return strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"))
35	}
36	
37	func formatToolInput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
38		args := tool.Input
39		switch normalizeToolName(tool.Name) {
40		case "execshell", "execshellwait", "execinteract", "execshellinteract",
41			"taskshellstart", "taskshellwait":
42			return formatExecuteInput(args)
43		case "readfile":
44			return renderReadInput(args)
45		case "listdir":
46			return renderListInput(args)
47		case "grepfiles":
48			return renderGrepInput(args)
49		case "filesearch":
50			return renderFileSearchInput(args)
51		case "websearch":
52			return renderWebSearchInput(args)
53		case "fetchurl":
54			return renderWebFetchInput(args)
55		case "writefile":
56			return renderWriteInput(args)
57		case "editfile":
58			return renderEditInput(args)
59		case "applypatch":
60			return renderApplyPatch(args)
61		case "todowrite", "updateplan", "checklistwrite":
62			return renderTodoWrite(args)
63		case "taskcreate", "taskread", "tasklist", "note":
64			return renderGenericJSON(args)
65		default:
66			return renderGenericJSON(args)
67		}
68	}
69	
70	func formatToolOutput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
71		if tool.Output == nil {
72			return ""
73		}
74		content, _ := tool.Output["content"].(string)
75		content = strings.TrimSpace(content)
76		if content == "" {
77			return ""
78		}
79		if strings.Contains(content, "\n") {
80			return fmt.Sprintf("Output:\n```text\n%s\n```", content)
81		}
82		return fmt.Sprintf("Output: %s", content)
83	}
84	
85	// extractPathHints surfaces filesystem paths referenced by a tool's input so
86	// downstream features (cloud sync, search) can index sessions by touched
87	// files. Mirrors droidcli's extractPathHints — same shell-command extraction
88	// via spi.ExtractShellPathHints.
89	func extractPathHints(input map[string]any, workspaceRoot string) []string {
90		if input == nil {
91			return nil
92		}
93	
94		pathFields := []string{
95			"path", "file_path", "filePath", "file",
96			"dir", "directory", "directory_path", "target_directory",
97			"workdir", "cwd", "target",
98		}
99		var hints []string
100		for _, field := range pathFields {
101			val, ok := input[field]
102			if !ok {
103				continue
104			}
105			switch v := val.(type) {
106			case string:
107				addPathHint(&hints, v, workspaceRoot)
108			case []any:
109				for _, entry := range v {
110					if s, ok := entry.(string); ok {
111						addPathHint(&hints, s, workspaceRoot)
112					}
113				}
114			}
115		}
116	
117		command, _ := input["command"].(string)
118		if command == "" {
119			command, _ = input["cmd"].(string)
120		}
121		if command != "" {
122			cwd, _ := input["workdir"].(string)
123			if cwd == "" {
124				cwd, _ = input["cwd"].(string)
125			}
126			if cwd == "" {
127				cwd = workspaceRoot
128			}
129			for _, sp := range spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot) {
130				addPathHint(&hints, sp, workspaceRoot)
131			}
132		}
133	
134		return hints
135	}
136	
137	func addPathHint(hints *[]string, value string, workspaceRoot string) {
138		value = strings.TrimSpace(value)
139		if value == "" {
140			return
141		}
142		normalized := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
143		for _, existing := range *hints {
144			if existing == normalized {
145				return
146			}
147		}
148		*hints = append(*hints, normalized)
149	}
150	
151	// --- per-tool input renderers ---
152	
153	func renderReadInput(args map[string]any) string {
154		path := stringValue(args, "file_path", "path", "file", "filePath")
155		if path == "" {
156			return renderGenericJSON(args)
157		}
158		var b strings.Builder
159		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Path: `%s`", path)
160		offset := stringValue(args, "offset", "start")
161		limit := stringValue(args, "limit", "lines", "max_lines")
162		if offset != "" || limit != "" {
163			if offset == "" {
164				offset = "0"
165			}
166			if limit == "" {
167				limit = "?"
168			}
169			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\nLines: offset %s, limit %s", offset, limit)
170		}
171		return b.String()
172	}
173	
174	func renderListInput(args map[string]any) string {
175		path := stringValue(args, "path", "dir", "directory", "directory_path", "target_directory")
176		if path == "" {
177			return renderGenericJSON(args)
178		}
179		return fmt.Sprintf("Path: `%s`", path)
180	}
181	
182	func renderGrepInput(args map[string]any) string {
183		parts := []string{}
184		if pat := stringValue(args, "pattern", "query", "regex"); pat != "" {
185			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Pattern: `%s`", pat))
186		}
187		if path := stringValue(args, "path", "dir", "directory"); path != "" {
188			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Path: `%s`", path))
189		}
190		if glob := stringValue(args, "include", "glob"); glob != "" {
191			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Glob: `%s`", glob))
192		}
193		if len(parts) == 0 {
194			return renderGenericJSON(args)
195		}
196		return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
197	}
198	
199	func renderFileSearchInput(args map[string]any) string {
200		if pat := stringValue(args, "pattern", "query", "name"); pat != "" {
201			return fmt.Sprintf("Pattern: `%s`", pat)
202		}
203		return renderGenericJSON(args)
204	}
205	
206	func renderWebSearchInput(args map[string]any) string {
207		if q := stringValue(args, "query", "q", "search"); q != "" {
208			return fmt.Sprintf("Query: `%s`", q)
209		}
210		return renderGenericJSON(args)
211	}
212	
213	func renderWebFetchInput(args map[string]any) string {
214		url := stringValue(args, "url", "uri")
215		prompt := stringValue(args, "prompt", "query")
216		if url == "" && prompt == "" {
217			return renderGenericJSON(args)
218		}
219		var parts []string
220		if url != "" {
221			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("URL: `%s`", url))
222		}
223		if prompt != "" {
224			parts = append(parts, prompt)
225		}
226		return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
227	}
228	
229	func renderWriteInput(args map[string]any) string {
230		path := stringValue(args, "file_path", "path", "file", "filePath")
231		content := stringValue(args, "content", "contents", "text", "data")
232		if path == "" && content == "" {
233			return renderGenericJSON(args)
234		}
235		var b strings.Builder
236		if path != "" {
237			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Path: `%s`", path)
238		}
239		if content != "" {
240			if b.Len() > 0 {
241				b.WriteString("\n\n")
242			}
243			b.WriteString(formatContentBlock(content, path))
244		}
245		return b.String()
246	}
247	
248	func renderEditInput(args map[string]any) string {
249		path := stringValue(args, "file_path", "path", "file", "filePath")
250		oldText := stringValue(args, "old_str", "old_text", "old_string", "old")
251		newText := stringValue(args, "new_str", "new_text", "new_string", "new")
252	
253		var b strings.Builder
254		if path != "" {
255			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Path: `%s`", path)
256		}
257		if oldText != "" || newText != "" {
258			if b.Len() > 0 {
259				b.WriteString("\n\n")
260			}
261			b.WriteString(formatDiffBlock(oldText, newText))
262		}
263		if b.Len() == 0 {
264			return renderGenericJSON(args)
265		}
266		return b.String()
267	}
268	
269	func renderApplyPatch(args map[string]any) string {
270		patch := stringValue(args, "patch", "input")
271		if patch == "" {
272			return renderGenericJSON(args)
273		}
274		return fmt.Sprintf("```diff\n%s\n```", strings.TrimSpace(patch))
275	}
276	
277	func renderTodoWrite(args map[string]any) string {
278		itemsRaw, ok := args["todos"].([]any)
279		if !ok || len(itemsRaw) == 0 {
280			// Fall back to plan/items keys used by update_plan / checklist_write.
281			for _, key := range []string{"items", "plan", "steps"} {
282				if v, ok := args[key].([]any); ok && len(v) > 0 {
283					itemsRaw = v
284					break
285				}
286			}
287		}
288		if len(itemsRaw) == 0 {
289			return renderGenericJSON(args)
290		}
291		var b strings.Builder
292		b.WriteString("Todo List:\n")
293		for _, raw := range itemsRaw {
294			item, _ := raw.(map[string]any)
295			status := strings.TrimSpace(stringValue(item, "status"))
296			desc := strings.TrimSpace(stringValue(item, "description", "content", "text"))
297			if desc == "" {
298				desc = "(no description)"
299			}
300			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- [%s] %s\n", todoSymbol(status), desc)
301		}
302		return strings.TrimRight(b.String(), "\n")
303	}
304	
305	func formatExecuteInput(args map[string]any) string {
306		command := stringValue(args, "command", "cmd")
307		workdir := stringValue(args, "workdir", "dir", "cwd")
308		if command == "" && workdir == "" {
309			return renderGenericJSON(args)
310		}
311		var b strings.Builder
312		if workdir != "" {
313			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Directory: `%s`\n\n", workdir)
314		}
315		if command != "" {
316			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "`%s`", command)
317		}
318		return b.String()
319	}
320	
321	func formatDiffBlock(oldText, newText string) string {
322		var b strings.Builder
323		b.WriteString("```diff\n")
324		for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
325			if oldText == "" {
326				break
327			}
328			b.WriteString("-")
329			b.WriteString(line)
330			b.WriteString("\n")
331		}
332		for _, line := range strings.Split(newText, "\n") {
333			if newText == "" {
334				break
335			}
336			b.WriteString("+")
337			b.WriteString(line)
338			b.WriteString("\n")
339		}
340		b.WriteString("```")
341		return b.String()
342	}
343	
344	func formatContentBlock(content, path string) string {
345		lang := languageFromPath(path)
346		escaped := strings.ReplaceAll(content, "```", "\\```")
347		if lang == "" {
348			return fmt.Sprintf("```\n%s\n```", escaped)
349		}
350		return fmt.Sprintf("```%s\n%s\n```", lang, escaped)
351	}
352	
353	func languageFromPath(path string) string {
354		if path == "" {
355			return ""
356		}
357		ext := strings.TrimPrefix(strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(path)), ".")
358		if ext == "" {
359			return ""
360		}
361		switch ext {
362		case "yml":
363			return "yaml"
364		case "md":
365			return "markdown"
366		default:
367			return ext
368		}
369	}
370	
371	// --- generic helpers (decoupled copy of droidcli's, scoped to this package) ---
372	
373	func renderGenericJSON(args map[string]any) string {
374		if len(args) == 0 {
375			return ""
376		}
377		keys := make([]string, 0, len(args))
378		for k := range args {
379			keys = append(keys, k)
380		}
381		sort.Strings(keys)
382		var b strings.Builder
383		b.WriteString("```json\n{")
384		for i, k := range keys {
385			if i > 0 {
386				b.WriteString(",")
387			}
388			b.WriteString("\n  \"")
389			b.WriteString(k)
390			b.WriteString("\": ")
391			b.WriteString(renderJSONValue(args[k]))
392		}
393		b.WriteString("\n}\n```")
394		return b.String()
395	}
396	
397	func renderJSONValue(v any) string {
398		switch val := v.(type) {
399		case string:
400			bytes, _ := json.Marshal(val)
401			return string(bytes)
402		case json.Number:
403			return val.String()
404		default:
405			bytes, err := json.Marshal(val)
406			if err != nil {
407				return fmt.Sprintf("%q", fmt.Sprint(val))
408			}
409			return string(bytes)
410		}
411	}
412	
413	func stringValue(args map[string]any, keys ...string) string {
414		for _, key := range keys {
415			val, ok := args[key]
416			if !ok {
417				continue
418			}
419			switch v := val.(type) {
420			case string:
421				return v
422			case json.Number:
423				return v.String()
424			case float64:
425				return strconv.FormatFloat(v, 'f', -1, 64)
426			case int:
427				return strconv.Itoa(v)
428			case int64:
429				return strconv.FormatInt(v, 10)
430			case bool:
431				return strconv.FormatBool(v)
432			}
433		}
434		return ""
435	}
436	
437	func normalizeToolName(name string) string {
438		cleaned := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
439		cleaned = strings.ReplaceAll(cleaned, " ", "")
440		cleaned = strings.ReplaceAll(cleaned, "-", "")
441		cleaned = strings.ReplaceAll(cleaned, "_", "")
442		return cleaned
443	}
444	
445	func todoSymbol(status string) string {
446		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(status)) {
447		case "completed", "done":
448			return "x"
449		case "in_progress", "active":
450			return "⚡"
451		default:
452			return " "
453		}
454	}
455
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go`

```text
1	package deepseek
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"sort"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		deepSeekRootDir     = ".deepseek"
16		deepSeekSessionsDir = "sessions"
17	)
18	
19	type sessionFile struct {
20		Path     string
21		ModTime  int64
22		FileName string
23	}
24	
25	// resolveSessionsDir returns the path to DeepSeek TUI's sessions directory.
26	func resolveSessionsDir() (string, error) {
27		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
28		if err != nil {
29			return "", fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot resolve home dir: %w", err)
30		}
31		return filepath.Join(home, deepSeekRootDir, deepSeekSessionsDir), nil
32	}
33	
34	// listSessionFiles returns all JSON session files in ~/.deepseek/sessions/,
35	// sorted by modification time (newest first).
36	func listSessionFiles() ([]sessionFile, error) {
37		dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
38		if err != nil {
39			return nil, err
40		}
41	
42		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
43		if err != nil {
44			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
45				return []sessionFile{}, nil
46			}
47			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: unable to read sessions dir: %w", err)
48		}
49	
50		var files []sessionFile
51		for _, entry := range entries {
52			if entry.IsDir() {
53				continue
54			}
55			if filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) != ".json" {
56				continue
57			}
58	
59			info, err := entry.Info()
60			if err != nil {
61				continue
62			}
63	
64			files = append(files, sessionFile{
65				Path:     filepath.Join(dir, entry.Name()),
66				ModTime:  info.ModTime().UnixNano(),
67				FileName: entry.Name(),
68			})
69		}
70	
71		// Sort newest first.
72		sort.Slice(files, func(i, j int) bool {
73			return files[i].ModTime > files[j].ModTime
74		})
75	
76		return files, nil
77	}
78	
79	// findSessionFileByID finds a session file by its ID (UUID).
80	// The ID should match a filename like <uuid>.json.
81	func findSessionFileByID(sessionID string) (string, error) {
82		files, err := listSessionFiles()
83		if err != nil {
84			return "", err
85		}
86	
87		for _, file := range files {
88			candidate := trimExt(file.FileName)
89			if candidate == sessionID || file.FileName == sessionID {
90				return file.Path, nil
91			}
92		}
93		return "", nil
94	}
95	
96	func trimExt(name string) string {
97		ext := filepath.Ext(name)
98		if ext == "" {
99			return name
100		}
101		return name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
102	}
103	
104	// sessionMentionsProject checks whether a session file's workspace metadata
105	// matches the given project path.
106	func sessionMentionsProject(filePath string, projectPath string) bool {
107		projectPath = strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
108		if projectPath == "" {
109			return false
110		}
111	
112		// Quick check: read only the metadata portion of the JSON to avoid
113		// parsing the full messages array.
114		workspace := extractWorkspaceFast(filePath)
115		if workspace == "" {
116			return false
117		}
118	
119		canonicalProject := canonicalizePath(projectPath)
120		canonicalWorkspace := canonicalizePath(workspace)
121	
122		return canonicalProject == canonicalWorkspace
123	}
124	
125	// extractWorkspaceFast reads just the workspace field from a session file
126	// without parsing the full JSON. The metadata block is at the top of the file,
127	// so we stream and bail on the first match — important on the watcher hot
128	// path where this is called per fs event.
129	func extractWorkspaceFast(filePath string) string {
130		f, err := os.Open(filePath)
131		if err != nil {
132			return ""
133		}
134		defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
135	
136		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)
137		// DeepSeek session files can contain individual lines well over the default
138		// 64KB scanner limit (long tool_result blobs); raise to 1MB which still
139		// bounds memory but covers everything seen in practice.
140		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 1024*1024)
141		for scanner.Scan() {
142			line := scanner.Text()
143			messagesIdx := strings.Index(line, `"messages"`)
144			searchLine := line
145			if messagesIdx >= 0 {
146				searchLine = line[:messagesIdx]
147			}
148			if ws := matchWorkspace(searchLine); ws != "" {
149				return ws
150			}
151			// Once we cross into the messages array the metadata is behind us.
152			if messagesIdx >= 0 {
153				return ""
154			}
155		}
156		return ""
157	}
158	
159	// matchWorkspace pulls the value out of a `"workspace": "<value>"` JSON line.
160	func matchWorkspace(line string) string {
161		idx := strings.Index(line, `"workspace"`)
162		if idx < 0 {
163			return ""
164		}
165		rest := line[idx+len(`"workspace"`):]
166		colon := strings.Index(rest, ":")
167		if colon < 0 {
168			return ""
169		}
170		rest = strings.TrimSpace(rest[colon+1:])
171		if len(rest) == 0 || rest[0] != '"' {
172			return ""
173		}
174		rest = rest[1:]
175		end := strings.Index(rest, `"`)
176		if end < 0 {
177			return ""
178		}
179		return rest[:end]
180	}
181	
182	// canonicalizePath resolves a path to its canonical form for comparison.
183	func canonicalizePath(path string) string {
184		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(path)
185		if trimmed == "" {
186			return ""
187		}
188		canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(trimmed)
189		if err == nil {
190			return canonical
191		}
192		if abs, err := filepath.Abs(trimmed); err == nil {
193			return filepath.Clean(abs)
194		}
195		return filepath.Clean(trimmed)
196	}
197
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/deepseek/watcher.go`

```text
1	package deepseek
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log/slog"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
12		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
13	)
14	
15	type watchState struct {
16		lastProcessed map[string]int64
17	}
18	
19	// watchSessions watches the DeepSeek TUI sessions directory for new or modified
20	// session files and invokes sessionCallback for each change.
21	func watchSessions(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
22		if sessionCallback == nil {
23			return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
24		}
25	
26		sessionsDir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
27		if err != nil {
28			return err
29		}
30	
31		watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
32		if err != nil {
33			return fmt.Errorf("deepseek: failed to create watcher: %w", err)
34		}
35		defer func() {
36			_ = watcher.Close()
37		}()
38	
39		state := &watchState{lastProcessed: make(map[string]int64)}
40	
41		// Initial scan to process existing sessions.
42		if err := scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state); err != nil {
43			slog.Debug("deepseek: initial scan failed", "error", err)
44		}
45	
46		// Set up watching on the sessions directory.
47		if err := setupWatcher(watcher, sessionsDir); err != nil {
48			slog.Debug("deepseek: setup watches failed", "error", err)
49		}
50	
51		// Event loop.
52		for {
53			select {
54			case <-ctx.Done():
55				return ctx.Err()
56			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
57				if !ok {
58					return nil
59				}
60				handleWatchEvent(event, watcher, sessionsDir, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
61			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
62				if !ok {
63					return nil
64				}
65				slog.Debug("deepseek: watcher error", "error", err)
66			}
67		}
68	}
69	
70	// setupWatcher adds a watch on the sessions directory.
71	// If the directory doesn't exist yet, it watches the parent .deepseek directory
72	// so we can detect when sessions/ is created.
73	func setupWatcher(watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string) error {
74		if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
75			// Watch parent .deepseek directory.
76			parentDir := filepath.Dir(sessionsDir)
77			if _, err := os.Stat(parentDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
78				homeDir := filepath.Dir(parentDir)
79				slog.Debug("deepseek: watching home for .deepseek creation", "path", homeDir)
80				return watcher.Add(homeDir)
81			}
82			slog.Debug("deepseek: watching .deepseek for sessions creation", "path", parentDir)
83			return watcher.Add(parentDir)
84		}
85	
86		slog.Debug("deepseek: watching sessions directory", "path", sessionsDir)
87		return watcher.Add(sessionsDir)
88	}
89	
90	// handleWatchEvent processes a filesystem event for new or modified session files.
91	func handleWatchEvent(event fsnotify.Event, watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
92		slog.Debug("deepseek: received fs event", "op", event.Op.String(), "path", event.Name)
93	
94		// Handle directory creation — add new watches.
95		if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
96			info, err := os.Stat(event.Name)
97			if err != nil {
98				return
99			}
100			if info.IsDir() {
101				// If sessions dir itself was just created, set up proper watch.
102				if event.Name == sessionsDir {
103					if err := setupWatcher(watcher, sessionsDir); err != nil {
104						slog.Debug("deepseek: failed to setup watch on new sessions dir", "error", err)
105					}
106					// Scan immediately for any pre-existing files.
107					if scanErr := scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state); scanErr != nil {
108						slog.Debug("deepseek: scan after new dir watch failed", "error", scanErr)
109					}
110				}
111				return
112			}
113		}
114	
115		// Only process .json files.
116		if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".json") {
117			return
118		}
119	
120		// Process on Create and Write events.
121		if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
122			return
123		}
124	
125		slog.Debug("deepseek: json file event", "op", event.Op.String(), "file", event.Name)
126		processSessionFile(event.Name, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
127	}
128	
129	// processSessionFile processes a single JSON session file if it has been modified.
130	func processSessionFile(filePath string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
131		info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
132		if err != nil {
133			return
134		}
135	
136		modTime := info.ModTime().UnixNano()
137		if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[filePath]; ok && last >= modTime {
138			return
139		}
140	
141		if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(filePath, projectPath) {
142			return
143		}
144	
145		session, err := parseSessionFile(filePath, true)
146		if err != nil {
147			slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", filePath, "error", err)
148			return
149		}
150	
151		chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
152		if chat == nil {
153			return
154		}
155	
156		state.lastProcessed[filePath] = modTime
157		dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
158	}
159	
160	// scanAndProcessSessions scans all session files and processes any that have been modified.
161	func scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) error {
162		files, err := listSessionFiles()
163		if err != nil {
164			return err
165		}
166	
167		for _, file := range files {
168			if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[file.Path]; ok && last >= file.ModTime {
169				continue
170			}
171			if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
172				continue
173			}
174			session, err := parseSessionFile(file.Path, true)
175			if err != nil {
176				slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
177				continue
178			}
179			chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
180			if chat == nil {
181				continue
182			}
183			state.lastProcessed[file.Path] = file.ModTime
184			dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
185		}
186		return nil
187	}
188	
189	// dispatchSession invokes the session callback in a goroutine with panic recovery.
190	func dispatchSession(sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
191		if sessionCallback == nil || session == nil {
192			return
193		}
194		go func() {
195			defer func() {
196				if r := recover(); r != nil {
197					slog.Error("deepseek: session callback panicked", "panic", r)
198				}
199			}()
200			sessionCallback(session)
201		}()
202	}
203
```

11	// TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps: re-parsing the same session must
12	// produce identical EndTimes. Fix: buildExchanges uses UpdatedAt (fallback
13	// CreatedAt) instead of time.Now() so output stays byte-stable.
14	func TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps(t *testing.T) {
15		session := &dsSession{
16			Metadata: dsMetadata{
17				ID:        "sess-stable",
18				CreatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z",
19				UpdatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z",
20			},
21			Messages: []dsMessage{
22				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hi"}}},
23				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hello"}}},
24			},
25		}
26	
27		first := buildExchanges(session, "")
28		second := buildExchanges(session, "")
29	
30		if len(first) != 1 || len(second) != 1 {
31			t.Fatalf("expected 1 exchange each, got %d / %d", len(first), len(second))
32		}
33		if first[0].EndTime != second[0].EndTime {
34			t.Errorf("EndTime not deterministic: %q vs %q", first[0].EndTime, second[0].EndTime)
35		}
36		if first[0].EndTime != "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z" {
37			t.Errorf("expected EndTime=UpdatedAt, got %q", first[0].EndTime)
38		}
39		if first[0].StartTime != "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z" {
40			t.Errorf("expected StartTime=CreatedAt, got %q", first[0].StartTime)

67	// TestAttachToolResults_ParallelToolCalls: when the assistant emits parallel
68	// tool calls (id=A, id=B) and the next user message contains tool_results for
69	// B then A, each result must land on the message owning its tool_use_id — not
70	// be concatenated onto whichever assistant tool message came last.
71	func TestAttachToolResults_ParallelToolCalls(t *testing.T) {
72		session := &dsSession{
73			Metadata: dsMetadata{
74				ID:        "sess-parallel",
75				CreatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z",
76				UpdatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z",
77			},
78			Messages: []dsMessage{
79				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "do two reads"}}},
80				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{
81					{Type: "tool_use", Name: "read_file", ID: "A", Input: map[string]interface{}{"file_path": "a.go"}},
82					{Type: "tool_use", Name: "read_file", ID: "B", Input: map[string]interface{}{"file_path": "b.go"}},
83				}},
84				// Results returned in REVERSE order to make sure we match by id, not order.
85				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{
86					{Type: "tool_result", ToolUseID: "B", ToolResultContent: "contents-of-B"},
87					{Type: "tool_result", ToolUseID: "A", ToolResultContent: "contents-of-A"},
88				}},
89			},
90		}
91	
92		exchanges := buildExchanges(session, "")
93		if len(exchanges) != 1 {
94			t.Fatalf("expected 1 exchange, got %d", len(exchanges))
95		}
96		msgs := exchanges[0].Messages
97		// user text + 2 tool_use messages = 3
98		if len(msgs) != 3 {
99			t.Fatalf("expected 3 messages, got %d", len(msgs))
100		}
101	
102		// Find each tool message by UseID.
103		byID := map[string]*Message{}
104		for i := range msgs {
105			if msgs[i].Tool != nil {
106				byID[msgs[i].Tool.UseID] = &msgs[i]
107			}
108		}
109		a, b := byID["A"], byID["B"]
110		if a == nil || b == nil {
111			t.Fatalf("missing tool messages: A=%v B=%v", a, b)
112		}
113	
114		gotA, _ := a.Tool.Output["content"].(string)
115		gotB, _ := b.Tool.Output["content"].(string)
116		if gotA != "contents-of-A" {
117			t.Errorf("tool A got wrong result: %q", gotA)
118		}
119		if gotB != "contents-of-B" {
120			t.Errorf("tool B got wrong result: %q", gotB)
121		}
122	
123		// Each FormattedMarkdown should now include its own Output.
124		if a.Tool.FormattedMarkdown == nil || !strings.Contains(*a.Tool.FormattedMarkdown, "contents-of-A") {
125			t.Errorf("tool A markdown missing its result: %v", a.Tool.FormattedMarkdown)
126		}
127		if b.Tool.FormattedMarkdown == nil || !strings.Contains(*b.Tool.FormattedMarkdown, "contents-of-B") {
128			t.Errorf("tool B markdown missing its result: %v", b.Tool.FormattedMarkdown)
129		}
130		// Critically: A's markdown should NOT contain B's result (the old bug).
131		if a.Tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.Contains(*a.Tool.FormattedMarkdown, "contents-of-B") {
132			t.Errorf("tool A leaked tool B's result (the bug we fixed): %s", *a.Tool.FormattedMarkdown)
133		}
134	}

390			{Type: "tool_use", Name: "read_file", ID: "read", Input: map[string]interface{}{"file_path": "/repo/pkg/main.go"}},
391			{Type: "tool_use", Name: "exec_shell", ID: "shell", Input: map[string]interface{}{"command": "echo hi > out.txt", "workdir": "/repo"}},
392		}}, "deepseek-r1", "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z", "/repo")
393	
394		if len(msgs) != 2 {
395			t.Fatalf("expected 2 tool messages, got %d", len(msgs))
396		}
397		if !reflect.DeepEqual(msgs[0].PathHints, []string{"pkg/main.go"}) {
398			t.Errorf("read_file PathHints = %v, want [pkg/main.go]", msgs[0].PathHints)
399		}
400		foundShellOutput := false
401		for _, hint := range msgs[1].PathHints {
402			if hint == "out.txt" {
403				foundShellOutput = true
404				break
405			}
406		}
407		if !foundShellOutput {
408			t.Errorf("exec_shell PathHints = %v, want to contain out.txt", msgs[1].PathHints)
409		}
410	}

436	func TestDeriveSlug(t *testing.T) {
437		tests := []struct {
438			name    string
439			session *dsSession
440			want    string // empty means "non-empty"; specific string means exact match
441		}{
442			{
443				name: "first user message becomes slug source",
444				session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{
445					{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "Help me refactor the parser"}}},
446				}},
447			},
448			{
449				name: "skips assistant messages and turn_meta blocks",
450				session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{
451					{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "ignore me"}}},
452					{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "<turn_meta>noise"}}},
453					{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "Real question here"}}},
454				}},
455			},
456			{
457				name:    "empty session yields default slug",
458				session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{}},
459				want:    "deepseek-session",
460			},
461			{
462				name: "no user messages yields default slug",
463				session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{
464					{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hi"}}},
465				}},
466				want: "deepseek-session",
467			},
468		}
469	
470		for _, tt := range tests {
471			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
472				got := deriveSlug(tt.session)
473				if tt.want != "" {
474					if got != tt.want {
475						t.Errorf("deriveSlug() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
476					}
477					return
478				}
479				if got == "" || got == "deepseek-session" {
480					t.Errorf("expected non-default slug, got %q", got)
481				}
482			})
483		}
484	}

486	// TestBuildExchanges_TwoTurns covers exchange grouping: a real user turn
487	// boundary should start a new exchange while tool-result-only user messages
488	// stay attached to the current assistant turn.
489	func TestBuildExchanges_TwoTurns(t *testing.T) {
490		session := &dsSession{
491			Metadata: dsMetadata{
492				ID:        "sess-2turn",
493				CreatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z",
494				UpdatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z",
495			},
496			Messages: []dsMessage{
497				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "first question"}}},
498				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{
499					{Type: "tool_use", Name: "read_file", ID: "A"},
500				}},
501				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{
502					{Type: "tool_result", ToolUseID: "A", ToolResultContent: "result"},
503				}},
504				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "answer one"}}},
505				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "second question"}}},
506				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "answer two"}}},
507			},
508		}
509	
510		exchanges := buildExchanges(session, "")
511		if len(exchanges) != 2 {
512			t.Fatalf("expected 2 exchanges, got %d", len(exchanges))
513		}
514	
515		// Exchange IDs should be deterministic and indexed.
516		if exchanges[0].ExchangeID != "sess-2turn:0" {
517			t.Errorf("expected first ExchangeID 'sess-2turn:0', got %q", exchanges[0].ExchangeID)
518		}
519		if exchanges[1].ExchangeID != "sess-2turn:1" {
520			t.Errorf("expected second ExchangeID 'sess-2turn:1', got %q", exchanges[1].ExchangeID)
521		}
522	}

11	func TestEnsureResumeArgs(t *testing.T) {
12		tests := []struct {
13			name            string
14			args            []string
15			resumeSessionID string
16			expected        []string
17		}{
18			{
19				name:            "returns args unchanged when resumeSessionID is empty",
20				args:            []string{"--verbose"},
21				resumeSessionID: "",
22				expected:        []string{"--verbose"},
23			},
24			{
25				name:            "returns args unchanged when --resume already has value",
26				args:            []string{"--resume", "existing-session"},
27				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
28				expected:        []string{"--resume", "existing-session"},
29			},
30			{
31				name:            "returns args unchanged when -r already has value",
32				args:            []string{"-r", "existing-session"},
33				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
34				expected:        []string{"-r", "existing-session"},
35			},
36			{
37				name:            "returns args unchanged when --resume=value exists",
38				args:            []string{"--resume=existing-session"},
39				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
40				expected:        []string{"--resume=existing-session"},
41			},
42			{
43				name:            "repairs dangling --resume",
44				args:            []string{"--resume"},
45				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
46				expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session"},
47			},
48			{
49				name:            "repairs dangling -r",
50				args:            []string{"-r"},
51				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
52				expected:        []string{"-r", "new-session"},
53			},
54			{
55				name:            "repairs --resume followed by another flag",
56				args:            []string{"--resume", "--verbose"},
57				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
58				expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session", "--verbose"},
59			},
60			{
61				name:            "repairs empty --resume equals form",
62				args:            []string{"--resume="},
63				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
64				expected:        []string{"--resume=new-session"},
65			},
66			{
67				name:            "appends --resume when not present",
68				args:            []string{"--verbose"},
69				resumeSessionID: "new-session",
70				expected:        []string{"--verbose", "--resume", "new-session"},
71			},
72			{
73				name:            "appends --resume when args is nil",
74				args:            nil,

76				expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session"},
77			},

96	func TestParseCommand(t *testing.T) {
97		tests := []struct {
98			name        string
99			input       string
100			wantCmd     string
101			wantArgs    []string
102			wantHomeFix bool
103		}{
104			{
105				name:     "empty input returns default",
106				input:    "",
107				wantCmd:  defaultCommand,
108				wantArgs: nil,
109			},
110			{
111				name:     "whitespace-only input returns default",
112				input:    "   ",
113				wantCmd:  defaultCommand,
114				wantArgs: nil,
115			},
116			{
117				name:     "single token returns command and no args",
118				input:    "/usr/local/bin/deepseek",
119				wantCmd:  "/usr/local/bin/deepseek",
120				wantArgs: []string{},
121			},
122			{
123				name:     "command plus args splits on whitespace",
124				input:    "deepseek --debug --workdir /tmp",
125				wantCmd:  "deepseek",
126				wantArgs: []string{"--debug", "--workdir", "/tmp"},
127			},
128			{
129				name:     "quoted custom command args are preserved",
130				input:    "deepseek --config \"/tmp/path with spaces/config.json\" --prompt 'hello world'",
131				wantCmd:  "deepseek",
132				wantArgs: []string{"--config", "/tmp/path with spaces/config.json", "--prompt", "hello world"},
133			},
134			{
135				name:        "leading tilde is expanded to home",
136				input:       "~/bin/deepseek --verbose",
137				wantArgs:    []string{"--verbose"},
138				wantHomeFix: true,
139			},
140		}
141	
142		home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
143	
144		for _, tt := range tests {
145			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
146				gotCmd, gotArgs := parseCommand(tt.input)
147	
148				if tt.wantHomeFix {
149					expected := filepath.Join(home, "bin/deepseek")
150					if gotCmd != expected {
151						t.Errorf("parseCommand() cmd = %q, want %q", gotCmd, expected)
152					}
153				} else if gotCmd != tt.wantCmd {
154					t.Errorf("parseCommand() cmd = %q, want %q", gotCmd, tt.wantCmd)
155				}
156	
157				if !reflect.DeepEqual(gotArgs, tt.wantArgs) {
158					t.Errorf("parseCommand() args = %v, want %v", gotArgs, tt.wantArgs)
159				}
160			})
161		}
162	}
163	
164	func TestParseCommand_DoesNotMutate(t *testing.T) {
165		// Guard against accidental in-place modification of the input string,
166		// which would surprise callers that re-use the same custom-command value.
167		input := "deepseek --foo"
168		original := input
169		_, _ = parseCommand(input)
170		if input != original {
171			t.Errorf("parseCommand mutated input: got %q, want %q", input, original)
172		}
173		// Also verify args slice is independent (we can mutate without aliasing into spi internals).
174		_, args := parseCommand("deepseek --a --b")
175		if len(args) > 0 {
176			args[0] = "MUTATED"
177			_, args2 := parseCommand("deepseek --a --b")
178			if strings.Contains(strings.Join(args2, " "), "MUTATED") {
179				t.Errorf("parseCommand returned aliased slice; got %v", args2)
180			}
181		}
182	}
183

8	// TestFormatToolCall_ReadFile_InputThenOutput exercises the two-phase render
9	// pattern: first a tool_use is rendered with input only (no Output map yet),
10	// then attachToolResults sets Output and the renderer adds an "Output:" block.
11	func TestFormatToolCall_ReadFile_InputThenOutput(t *testing.T) {
12		tool := &ToolInfo{
13			Name:  "read_file",
14			Type:  "read",
15			UseID: "id-1",
16			Input: map[string]interface{}{"file_path": "pkg/main.go"},
17		}
18	
19		// Phase 1: input only, no result yet.
20		got := formatToolCall(tool)
21		if !strings.Contains(got, "Path: `pkg/main.go`") {
22			t.Errorf("phase 1 missing input section, got:\n%s", got)
23		}
24		if strings.Contains(got, "Output:") {
25			t.Errorf("phase 1 should not have Output yet, got:\n%s", got)
26		}
27	
28		// Phase 2: result attached.
29		tool.Output = map[string]interface{}{"content": "package main\n\nfunc main() {}"}
30		got = formatToolCall(tool)
31		if !strings.Contains(got, "Path: `pkg/main.go`") {
32			t.Errorf("phase 2 lost input section, got:\n%s", got)
33		}
34		if !strings.Contains(got, "Output:") {
35			t.Errorf("phase 2 should now include Output block, got:\n%s", got)
36		}
37		if !strings.Contains(got, "package main") {
38			t.Errorf("phase 2 should include actual content, got:\n%s", got)
39		}
40		// Multi-line output gets fenced with ```text.
41		if !strings.Contains(got, "```text") {
42			t.Errorf("multi-line output should use ```text fence, got:\n%s", got)
43		}
44	}

299				input: map[string]interface{}{
300					"path": []interface{}{"a.go", "b.go"},
301				},
302				wantContains: []string{"a.go", "b.go"},
303			},
304		}
305	
306		for _, tt := range tests {
307			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
308				got := extractPathHints(tt.input, tt.workspaceRoot)
309	
310				if len(tt.wantContains) == 0 {
311					if len(got) != 0 {
312						t.Errorf("expected no hints, got %v", got)
313					}
314					return
315				}
316	
317				for _, want := range tt.wantContains {
318					found := false
319					for _, h := range got {
320						if h == want {
321							found = true
322							break
323						}
324					}
325					if !found {
326						t.Errorf("expected hints to contain %q, got %v", want, got)
327					}
328				}
329	
330				// Also assert no duplicates
331				seen := make(map[string]int)
332				for _, h := range got {
333					seen[h]++
334					if seen[h] > 1 {
335						t.Errorf("duplicate hint %q in %v", h, got)
336					}
337				}
338	
339				for _, notWant := range tt.wantNot {
340					for _, h := range got {
341						if h == notWant {
342							t.Errorf("hint %q should not be present, got %v", notWant, got)
343						}
344					}
345				}
346			})
347		}
348	}
349
