```text
9	
10	// TestExtractWorkspaceFast covers the streaming workspace extraction. The
11	// function must:
12	//  1. Return the workspace value when it appears in metadata before "messages".
13	//  2. NOT pick up "workspace" strings that appear inside tool_result content
14	//     after the "messages" key has been seen — those are unrelated noise.
15	//  3. Return "" when no workspace is present at all.
16	//  4. Handle very large lines (the buffer was raised to 1MB for tool blobs).
17	func TestExtractWorkspaceFast(t *testing.T) {
18		tests := []struct {
19			name    string
20			content string
21			want    string
22		}{
23			{
24				name: "workspace before messages key",
25				content: `{
26	  "schema_version": 1,
27	  "metadata": {
28	    "id": "sess-1",
29	    "workspace": "/Users/me/proj"
30	  },
31	  "messages": []
32	}`,
33				want: "/Users/me/proj",
34			},
35			{
36				name: "workspace key only inside tool_result after messages is ignored",
37				content: `{
38	  "metadata": {"id": "sess-1"},
39	  "messages": [
40	    {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "content": "this string mentions \"workspace\": \"/fake\""}]}
41	  ]
42	}`,
43				want: "",
44			},
45			{
46				name: "no workspace key at all",
47				content: `{
48	  "metadata": {"id": "sess-2"},
49	  "messages": []
50	}`,
51				want: "",
52			},
53			{
54				name: "workspace on same line as messages key still found if it appears first",
55				// matchWorkspace runs before the messages bail-out, so a single-line
56				// JSON with workspace before messages should still resolve.
57				content: `{"metadata":{"workspace":"/single/line/path"},"messages":[]}`,
58				want:    "/single/line/path",
59			},
60			{
61				name:    "workspace-like text after messages on same line is ignored",
62				content: `{"metadata":{"id":"sess-1"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","content":"this string mentions \"workspace\": \"/fake\""}]}]}`,
63				want:    "",
64			},
65			{
66				name: "empty workspace value yields empty result",
67				content: `{
68	  "metadata": {"workspace": ""},
69	  "messages": []
70	}`,
71				want: "",
72			},
73		}
74	
75		for _, tt := range tests {
76			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
77				path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "session.json")
78				if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(tt.content), 0o644); err != nil {
79					t.Fatalf("write temp file: %v", err)
80				}
81				got := extractWorkspaceFast(path)
82				if got != tt.want {
83					t.Errorf("extractWorkspaceFast() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
84				}
85			})
86		}
87	}
88	
89	// TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine confirms the scanner's 1MB buffer handles
90	// tool_result blobs that exceed bufio.Scanner's default 64KB line cap. Without
91	// the raise, the scanner would return an error and the workspace (which sits
92	// before the giant line) would have been read fine — but if the workspace
93	// itself were on a long metadata line we'd lose it. Construct a case where the
94	// workspace lives on a long line to exercise the buffer.
95	func TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine(t *testing.T) {
96		// Build a metadata line that's ~200KB long but still valid JSON parseable
97		// by our quick matcher (we only scan textually).
98		bigComment := strings.Repeat("x", 200*1024)
99		content := `{"metadata":{"note":"` + bigComment + `","workspace":"/big/line/path"},"messages":[]}`
100	
101		path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "session.json")
102		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
103			t.Fatalf("write temp file: %v", err)
104		}
105		got := extractWorkspaceFast(path)
106		if got != "/big/line/path" {
107			t.Errorf("extractWorkspaceFast() = %q, want /big/line/path", got)
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// TestExtractWorkspaceFast_MissingFile returns empty string instead of erroring.
112	func TestExtractWorkspaceFast_MissingFile(t *testing.T) {
113		got := extractWorkspaceFast(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "no-such-file.json"))
114		if got != "" {
115			t.Errorf("expected empty string for missing file, got %q", got)
116		}
117	}

119	func TestSessionMentionsProject(t *testing.T) {

122			makeContent func(projectDir, otherDir string) string
123			projectDir  func(projectDir, otherDir string) string
124			want        bool

130				},
131				projectDir: func(p, _ string) string { return p },
132				want:       true,
133			},

135				name: "no match when workspace differs",

138				},
139				projectDir: func(p, _ string) string { return p },
140				want:       false,
141			},

143				name: "no match when no workspace key at all",

145					return `{"metadata":{"id":"x"},"messages":[]}`
146				},
147				projectDir: func(p, _ string) string { return p },
148				want:       false,
149			},
150			{
151				name: "empty project path returns false",
152				makeContent: func(p, _ string) string {
153					return `{"metadata":{"workspace":"` + p + `"},"messages":[]}`
154				},
155				projectDir: func(_, _ string) string { return "   " },
156				want:       false,
157			},
158		}
159	
160		for _, tt := range tests {
161			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
162				projectDir := t.TempDir()
163				otherDir := t.TempDir()
164				path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "session.json")
165				if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(tt.makeContent(projectDir, otherDir)), 0o644); err != nil {
166					t.Fatalf("write file: %v", err)
167				}
168				got := sessionMentionsProject(path, tt.projectDir(projectDir, otherDir))
169				if got != tt.want {
170					t.Errorf("sessionMentionsProject() = %v, want %v", got, tt.want)

176	func TestCanonicalizePath(t *testing.T) {
177		// Use real temp dirs so canonicalization (which calls filepath.EvalSymlinks
178		// under the hood) can resolve them. We can't assert exact strings on macOS
179		// because /var → /private/var, so we assert that equivalent inputs produce
180		// equivalent outputs and that empty input returns empty.
181		t.Run("empty input returns empty", func(t *testing.T) {
182			if got := canonicalizePath(""); got != "" {
183				t.Errorf("expected empty, got %q", got)
184			}
185			if got := canonicalizePath("   "); got != "" {
186				t.Errorf("expected empty for whitespace, got %q", got)
187			}
188		})
189	
190		t.Run("equivalent paths canonicalize to same value", func(t *testing.T) {
191			dir := t.TempDir()
192			// Same dir referenced two ways must canonicalize identically.
193			canonical := canonicalizePath(dir)
194			canonicalAgain := canonicalizePath(dir + "/")
195			if canonical != canonicalAgain {
196				t.Errorf("trailing slash changed canonical form: %q vs %q", canonical, canonicalAgain)
197			}
198			if canonical == "" {
199				t.Errorf("expected non-empty canonical for %q", dir)
200			}
201		})
202	
203		t.Run("nonexistent path still canonicalizes to absolute form", func(t *testing.T) {
204			got := canonicalizePath("/this/path/definitely/does/not/exist/xyz")
205			if !filepath.IsAbs(got) {
206				t.Errorf("expected absolute path for nonexistent input, got %q", got)
207			}
208		})
209	}
```

```text
10	func TestClassifyCheckError(t *testing.T) {

29				want: "permission_denied",
```

 `./pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

```text
1	// Package factory provides the provider registry and factory implementation.
2	// This package imports all concrete provider implementations and manages
3	// their registration, avoiding circular dependencies in the SPI layer.
4	package factory
5	
6	import (
7		"fmt"
8		"log/slog"
9		"sort"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli"
15		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli"
16		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek"
17		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli"
18		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli"
19		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
20	)
21	
22	// Registry manages all registered providers
23	type Registry struct {
24		providers         map[string]spi.Provider // key is the provider ID (e.g., "claude", "cursor")
25		mu                sync.RWMutex
26		initialized       bool
27		providerListCache string    // Cached formatted provider list string
28		providerListOnce  sync.Once // Ensures provider list is built only once
29	}
30	
31	// Global registry instance
32	var (
33		registry = &Registry{
34			providers: make(map[string]spi.Provider),
35		}
36		once sync.Once
37	)
38	
39	// ensureInitialized makes sure providers are registered
40	func (r *Registry) ensureInitialized() {
41		once.Do(func() {
42			r.registerAll()
43		})
44	}
45	
46	// registerAll registers all known providers.
47	// This is the ONLY place that needs to be updated when adding new providers.
48	func (r *Registry) registerAll() {
49		r.mu.Lock()
50		defer r.mu.Unlock()
51	
52		if r.initialized {
53			slog.Debug("Provider registry already initialized, skipping registration")
54			return
55		}
56	
57		slog.Debug("Initializing provider registry")
58	
59		// Register providers with simple IDs
60		// Each provider directly implements the spi.Provider interface
61		claudeProvider := claudecode.NewProvider()
62		r.providers["claude"] = claudeProvider
63		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "claude", "name", claudeProvider.Name())
64	
65		cursorProvider := cursorcli.NewProvider()
66		r.providers["cursor"] = cursorProvider
67		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "cursor", "name", cursorProvider.Name())
68	
69		codexProvider := codexcli.NewProvider()
70		r.providers["codex"] = codexProvider
71		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "codex", "name", codexProvider.Name())
72	
73		geminiProvider := geminicli.NewProvider()
74		r.providers["gemini"] = geminiProvider
75		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "gemini", "name", geminiProvider.Name())
76	
77		droidProvider := droidcli.NewProvider()
78		r.providers["droid"] = droidProvider
79		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "droid", "name", droidProvider.Name())
80	
81		deepseekProvider := deepseek.NewProvider()
82		r.providers["deepseek"] = deepseekProvider
83		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "deepseek", "name", deepseekProvider.Name())
84	
85		r.initialized = true
86		slog.Info("Provider registry initialized", "count", len(r.providers), "providers", r.ListIDsUnsafe())
87	}
88	
89	// ListIDsUnsafe returns provider IDs without locking (for internal use only)
90	func (r *Registry) ListIDsUnsafe() []string {
91		ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
92		for id := range r.providers {
93			ids = append(ids, id)
94		}
95		sort.Strings(ids)
96		return ids
97	}
98	
99	// GetRegistry returns the global registry instance
100	func GetRegistry() *Registry {
101		registry.ensureInitialized()
102		return registry
103	}
104	
105	// Get retrieves a provider by ID (case-insensitive)
106	func (r *Registry) Get(id string) (spi.Provider, error) {
107		r.ensureInitialized()
108		r.mu.RLock()
109		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
110	
111		// Case-insensitive lookup
112		for provID, provider := range r.providers {
113			if strings.EqualFold(provID, id) {
114				slog.Debug("Retrieved provider", "requested_id", id, "matched_id", provID, "name", provider.Name())
115				return provider, nil
116			}
117		}
118	
119		// Log available providers when lookup fails to aid debugging
120		availableIDs := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
121		for id := range r.providers {
122			availableIDs = append(availableIDs, id)
123		}
124		sort.Strings(availableIDs)
125	
126		slog.Warn("Provider not found",
127			"requested_id", id,
128			"available_providers", availableIDs)
129	
130		return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider '%s' not found", id)
131	}
132	
133	// GetAll returns all registered providers as a map of ID to Provider
134	func (r *Registry) GetAll() map[string]spi.Provider {
135		r.ensureInitialized()
136		r.mu.RLock()
137		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
138	
139		// Return a copy to prevent external modification
140		result := make(map[string]spi.Provider)
141		for id, provider := range r.providers {
142			result[id] = provider
143		}
144	
145		return result
146	}
147	
148	// ListIDs returns all registered provider IDs (sorted)
149	func (r *Registry) ListIDs() []string {
150		r.ensureInitialized()
151		r.mu.RLock()
152		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
153	
154		ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
155		for id := range r.providers {
156			ids = append(ids, id)
157		}
158	
159		sort.Strings(ids)
160		return ids
161	}
162	
163	// GetDefault returns the default provider (Claude)
164	func (r *Registry) GetDefault() (spi.Provider, error) {
165		r.ensureInitialized()
166		slog.Debug("Getting default provider (claude)")
167		return r.Get("claude")
168	}
169	
170	// GetProviderList returns a formatted string listing all providers.
171	// Used for help text and error messages.
172	// The result is cached since providers are static for the lifetime of the process.
173	func (r *Registry) GetProviderList() string {
174		r.ensureInitialized()
175	
176		// Build the provider list only once and cache it
177		r.providerListOnce.Do(func() {
178			r.mu.RLock()
179			defer r.mu.RUnlock()
180	
181			if len(r.providers) == 0 {
182				r.providerListCache = "No providers registered"
183				return
184			}
185	
186			// Get sorted IDs for consistent ordering
187			ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
188			for id := range r.providers {
189				ids = append(ids, id)
190			}
191			sort.Strings(ids)
192	
193			// Build the formatted list by accessing providers directly (no Get() calls)
194			var parts []string
195			for _, id := range ids {
196				if provider, ok := r.providers[id]; ok {
197					parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", id, provider.Name()))
198				}
199			}
200	
201			r.providerListCache = strings.Join(parts, ", ")
202		})
203	
204		return r.providerListCache
205	}
206
```

```text
1	// Package config provides configuration management for the SpecStory CLI.
2	// Configuration is loaded with the following priority (highest to lowest):
3	//  1. CLI flags
4	//  2. Local project config: .specstory/cli/config.toml
5	//  3. User-level config: ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
6	//
7	// Note: For telemetry settings, environment variables (OTEL_*) take highest priority
8	// per OpenTelemetry conventions.
9	package config
10	
11	import (
12		"fmt"
13		"log/slog"
14		"os"
15		"path/filepath"
16		"strings"
17	
18		"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
19	)
20	
21	const (
22		// ConfigFileName is the name of the configuration file
23		ConfigFileName = "config.toml"
24		// SpecStoryDir is the directory name for SpecStory files
25		SpecStoryDir = ".specstory"
26		// CLIDir is the subdirectory for CLI-specific files (config, auth, etc.)
27		CLIDir = "cli"
28	)
29	
30	// defaultConfigTemplate is the content written to a newly created config file.
31	// All options are commented out so the file is self-documenting but inert.
32	const defaultConfigTemplate = `# SpecStory CLI Configuration
33	#
34	# {u This is the user-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
35	# All settings here apply to every project unless overridden
36	# by a project-level config at ./.specstory/cli/config.toml
37	# or overridden by CLI flags.}
38	# {p This is the project-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
39	# All settings here apply to this project unless overridden by CLI flags.}
40	#
41	# Uncomment (remove the #) the line and edit any setting below to change the default behavior.
42	# For more information, see: https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage
43	
44	[local_sync]
45	# Write markdown files locally. (default: true)
46	# enabled = false # equivalent to --only-cloud-sync
47	
48	# Custom output directory for markdown files.
49	# Default: ./.specstory/history (relative to the project directory)
50	# output_dir = "~/.specstory/history" # equivalent to --output-dir "~/.specstory/history"
51	
52	# Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (default: false, UTC)
53	# local_time_zone = true # equivalent to --local-time-zone
54	
55	[cloud_sync]
56	# Sync session data to SpecStory Cloud. (default: true, when logged in to SpecStory Cloud)
57	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-cloud-sync
58	
59	[logging]
60	# Write logs to .specstory/debug/debug.log (default: false)
61	# log = true # equivalent to --log        
62	
63	# Debug-level output, requires console or log (default: false)
64	# debug = true # equivalent to --debug 
65	
66	# Custom output directory for debug data.
67	# Default: ./.specstory/debug (relative to the project directory)
68	# debug_dir = "~/.specstory/debug" # equivalent to --debug-dir "~/.specstory/debug"
69	
70	# Error/warn/info output to stdout (default: false)
71	# console = true # equivalent to --console
72	
73	# Suppress all non-error output (default: false)
74	# silent = true	# equivalent to --silent
75	
76	[version_check]
77	# Check for new versions of the CLI on startup.
78	# Default: true
79	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-version-check
80	
81	[analytics]
82	# Send anonymous product usage analytics to help improve SpecStory.
83	# Default: true
84	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-usage-analytics
85	
86	[telemetry]
87	# OTLP gRPC collector endpoint (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
88	# endpoint = "localhost:4317"
89	
90	# Override the default service name (default: "specstory-cli")
91	# service_name = "my-service-name"
92	
93	# Include user prompt text in telemetry spans (default: true)
94	# prompts = false
95	
96	[providers]
97	# Agent execution commands by provider (used by specstory run)
98	# Pass custom flags (e.g. claude_cmd = "claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions")
99	# Use of these is equivalent to -c "custom command"
100	
101	# Claude Code command
102	# claude_cmd = "claude"
103	
104	# Codex CLI command
105	# codex_cmd = "codex"
106	
107	# Cursor CLI command
108	# cursor_cmd = "cursor-agent"
109	
110	# Droid CLI command
111	# droid_cmd = "droid"
112	
113	# Gemini CLI command
114	# gemini_cmd = "gemini"
115	
116	# DeepSeek TUI command
117	# deepseek_cmd = "deepseek"
118	`
119	
120	// Config represents the complete CLI configuration
121	type Config struct {
122		VersionCheck VersionCheckConfig `toml:"version_check"`
123		CloudSync    CloudSyncConfig    `toml:"cloud_sync"`
124		LocalSync    LocalSyncConfig    `toml:"local_sync"`
125		Logging      LoggingConfig      `toml:"logging"`
126		Analytics    AnalyticsConfig    `toml:"analytics"`
127		Telemetry    TelemetryConfig    `toml:"telemetry"`
128		Providers    ProvidersConfig    `toml:"providers"`
129	}
130	
131	// VersionCheckConfig holds version check settings
132	type VersionCheckConfig struct {
133		// Enabled controls whether to check for newer versions on startup
134		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
135	}
136	
137	// CloudSyncConfig holds cloud sync settings
138	type CloudSyncConfig struct {
139		// Enabled controls whether cloud sync is active
140		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
141	}
142	
143	// LocalSyncConfig holds local file sync settings
144	type LocalSyncConfig struct {
145		// Enabled controls whether local markdown files are written
146		// When false, only cloud sync is performed (equivalent to --only-cloud-sync)
147		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
148		// OutputDir is the custom output directory for markdown files
149		OutputDir string `toml:"output_dir"`
150		// LocalTimeZone enables local timezone for file name and content timestamps
151		LocalTimeZone *bool `toml:"local_time_zone"`
152	}
153	
154	// LoggingConfig holds logging settings
155	type LoggingConfig struct {
156		// DebugDir is the custom output directory for debug data
157		DebugDir string `toml:"debug_dir"`
158		// Console enables error/warn/info output to stdout
159		Console *bool `toml:"console"`
160		// Log enables writing error/warn/info output to .specstory/debug/debug.log
161		Log *bool `toml:"log"`
162		// Debug enables debug-level output (requires Console or Log)
163		Debug *bool `toml:"debug"`
164		// Silent suppresses all non-error output
165		Silent *bool `toml:"silent"`
166	}
167	
168	// AnalyticsConfig holds analytics settings
169	type AnalyticsConfig struct {
170		// Enabled controls whether usage analytics are sent
171		Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
172	}
173	
174	// TelemetryConfig holds OpenTelemetry configuration.
175	// Telemetry is enabled when Endpoint is non-empty (no separate "enabled" flag).
176	type TelemetryConfig struct {
177		// Endpoint is the OTLP gRPC collector address (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
178		// Env var: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
179		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint"`
180	
181		// ServiceName overrides the default service name ("specstory-cli")
182		// Env var: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
183		ServiceName string `toml:"service_name"`
184	
185		// Prompts controls whether user prompt text is included in telemetry spans.
186		// Defaults to true (prompts are included). Set to false to exclude prompt text
187		// from the specstory.exchange.prompt_text attribute for privacy.
188		Prompts *bool `toml:"prompts"`
189	}
190	
191	// ProvidersConfig holds custom agent execution commands by provider.
192	// These are used by `specstory run` as the equivalent of the -c flag,
193	// scoped to a specific provider.
194	type ProvidersConfig struct {
195		ClaudeCmd   string `toml:"claude_cmd"`
196		CodexCmd    string `toml:"codex_cmd"`
197		CursorCmd   string `toml:"cursor_cmd"`
198		DeepSeekCmd string `toml:"deepseek_cmd"`
199		DroidCmd    string `toml:"droid_cmd"`
200		GeminiCmd   string `toml:"gemini_cmd"`
201	}
202	
203	// CLIOverrides holds CLI flag values that override config file settings.
204	// These are applied after config files are loaded.
205	type CLIOverrides struct {
206		// General
207		OutputDir     string
208		LocalTimeZone bool
209	
210		// Version check
211		NoVersionCheck bool
212	
213		// Cloud sync
214		NoCloudSync   bool
215		OnlyCloudSync bool
216	
217		// Logging
218		DebugDir string
219		Console  bool
220		Log      bool
221		Debug    bool
222		Silent   bool
223	
224		// Analytics
225		NoAnalytics bool
226	
227		// Telemetry
228		TelemetryEndpoint    string
229		TelemetryServiceName string
230		NoTelemetryPrompts   bool
231	}
232	
233	// Load reads configuration from files and CLI flags.
234	// Priority: CLI flags > local project config > user-level config
235	// Note: For telemetry, OTEL_* env vars take highest priority per OTel conventions.
236	//
237	// Returns an error if a config file exists but cannot be parsed (TOML syntax error)
238	// or cannot be read (permission denied, I/O error). Missing config files are not
239	// treated as errors - they are simply skipped.
240	func Load(cliOverrides *CLIOverrides) (*Config, error) {
241		cfg := &Config{}
242	
243		// Load user-level config first (lowest priority)
244		userConfigPath := getUserConfigPath()
245		if userConfigPath != "" {
246			if err := loadTOMLFile(userConfigPath, cfg); err != nil {
247				if os.IsNotExist(err) {
248					slog.Debug("No user-level config file found", "path", userConfigPath)
249					ensureDefaultUserConfig(userConfigPath)
250				} else {
251					// Parse error or permission denied - return error to caller
252					return cfg, fmt.Errorf("failed to load user config %s: %w", userConfigPath, err)
253				}
254			} else {
255				slog.Debug("Loaded user-level config", "path", userConfigPath)
256			}
257		}
258	
259		// Load local project config (overwrites user-level)
260		localConfigPath := getLocalConfigPath()
261		if localConfigPath != "" {
262			if err := loadTOMLFile(localConfigPath, cfg); err != nil {
263				if os.IsNotExist(err) {
264					slog.Debug("No local project config file found", "path", localConfigPath)
265				} else {
266					// Parse error or permission denied - return error to caller
267					return cfg, fmt.Errorf("failed to load project config %s: %w", localConfigPath, err)
268				}
269			} else {
270				slog.Debug("Loaded local project config", "path", localConfigPath)
271			}
272		}
273	
274		// Apply CLI flag overrides (highest priority for most settings)
275		if cliOverrides != nil {
276			applyCLIOverrides(cfg, cliOverrides)
277		}
278	
279		// Apply OTel environment variables (highest priority for telemetry only)
280		applyTelemetryEnvOverrides(cfg)
281	
282		return cfg, nil
283	}
284	
285	// GetUserConfigPath returns the path to ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
286	func GetUserConfigPath() string {
287		return getUserConfigPath()
288	}
289	
290	// GetLocalConfigPath returns the path to .specstory/cli/config.toml in the current directory
291	func GetLocalConfigPath() string {
292		return getLocalConfigPath()
293	}
294	
295	// getUserConfigPath returns the path to ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
296	func getUserConfigPath() string {
297		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
298		if err != nil {
299			slog.Debug("Could not determine home directory", "error", err)
300			return ""
301		}
302		return filepath.Join(home, SpecStoryDir, CLIDir, ConfigFileName)
303	}
304	
305	// getLocalConfigPath returns the path to .specstory/cli/config.toml in the current directory
306	func getLocalConfigPath() string {
307		cwd, err := os.Getwd()
308		if err != nil {
309			slog.Debug("Could not determine current directory", "error", err)
310			return ""
311		}
312		return filepath.Join(cwd, SpecStoryDir, CLIDir, ConfigFileName)
313	}
314	
315	// processTemplate processes the default config template for a given level
316	// ("user" or "project"). Template markers {u ...} and {p ...} delimit content
317	// specific to user-level or project-level config files respectively.
318	//
319	// For level "user": content inside {u ...} is kept (markers stripped),
320	// content inside {p ...} is removed entirely.
321	// For level "project": the opposite applies.
322	//
323	// Markers can span multiple lines. The opening marker ({u or {p) appears at
324	// the start of a line (after optional whitespace/comment prefix), and the
325	// closing } appears at the end of a line.
326	func processTemplate(template, level string) string {
327		var result strings.Builder
328		keepTag := "{u"
329		stripTag := "{p"
330		if level == "project" {
331			keepTag = "{p"
332			stripTag = "{u"
333		}
334	
335		insideKeep := false
336		insideStrip := false
337	
338		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(template, "\n") {
339			trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
340			// Remove any leading "# " to inspect the marker
341			bare := strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "# ")
342	
343			switch {
344			case insideStrip:
345				// Check for closing brace — ends the stripped block
346				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
347					insideStrip = false
348				}
349				// Drop the entire line regardless
350				continue
351	
352			case insideKeep:
353				// Check for closing brace — ends the kept block
354				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
355					insideKeep = false
356					// Strip the trailing } from the line
357					idx := strings.LastIndex(line, "}")
358					line = line[:idx]
359					// Only emit if something remains after stripping
360					if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(line) == "#" {
361						continue
362					}
363				}
364				result.WriteString(line)
365				result.WriteString("\n")
366	
367			case strings.HasPrefix(bare, keepTag+" "):
368				insideKeep = true
369				// prefix is everything before the bare marker (e.g. "# ")
370				prefix := line[:strings.Index(line, bare)]
371				// Check if single-line block (closing } on same line)
372				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
373					insideKeep = false
374					// Extract content between tag and closing brace
375					content := strings.TrimSpace(bare[len(keepTag)+1 : len(bare)-1])
376					reconstructed := prefix + content
377					if strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "#" {
378						continue
379					}
380					result.WriteString(reconstructed)
381					result.WriteString("\n")
382				} else {
383					// Multi-line: strip the marker prefix, keep content after it
384					content := strings.TrimSpace(bare[len(keepTag)+1:])
385					reconstructed := prefix + content
386					if strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "#" {
387						continue
388					}
389					result.WriteString(reconstructed)
390					result.WriteString("\n")
391				}
392	
393			case strings.HasPrefix(bare, stripTag+" "):
394				insideStrip = true
395				// Check if single-line block (closing } on same line)
396				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
397					insideStrip = false
398				}
399				continue
400	
401			default:
402				result.WriteString(line)
403				result.WriteString("\n")
404			}
405		}
406	
407		// Remove trailing extra newline that accumulates from the split
408		out := result.String()
409		if strings.HasSuffix(out, "\n\n") && !strings.HasSuffix(template, "\n\n") {
410			out = out[:len(out)-1]
411		}
412		return out
413	}
414	
415	// ensureDefaultUserConfig creates a default user-level config file with all
416	// options commented out. This makes the config discoverable without changing
417	// any behavior. Failures are silently ignored — this is a convenience, not
418	// a requirement.
419	func ensureDefaultUserConfig(path string) {
420		dir := filepath.Dir(path)
421		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
422			slog.Debug("Could not create config directory", "path", dir, "error", err)
423			return
424		}
425		processed := processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user")
426		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(processed), 0644); err != nil {
427			slog.Debug("Could not write default config file", "path", path, "error", err)
428			return
429		}
430		slog.Debug("Created default user config file", "path", path)
431	}
432	
433	// EnsureDefaultProjectConfig creates a default project-level config file at
434	// .specstory/cli/config.toml if one doesn't already exist. All options are
435	// commented out so the file is self-documenting but inert.
436	//
437	// This should only be called from commands that imply active project work
438	// (run, sync, watch) to avoid scattering config files in arbitrary directories.
439	// Failures are silently ignored — this is a convenience, not a requirement.
440	func EnsureDefaultProjectConfig() {
441		path := getLocalConfigPath()
442		if path == "" {
443			return
444		}
445		// Don't overwrite an existing file
446		if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
447			return
448		}
449		dir := filepath.Dir(path)
450		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
451			slog.Debug("Could not create config directory", "path", dir, "error", err)
452			return
453		}
454		processed := processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "project")
455		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(processed), 0644); err != nil {
456			slog.Debug("Could not write default config file", "path", path, "error", err)
457			return
458		}
459		slog.Debug("Created default project config file", "path", path)
460	}
461	
462	// loadTOMLFile reads a TOML file and decodes it into the config struct.
463	// Fields are merged (later calls overwrite earlier values).
464	func loadTOMLFile(path string, cfg *Config) error {
465		_, err := toml.DecodeFile(path, cfg)
466		return err
467	}
468	
469	// applyTelemetryEnvOverrides applies OTel environment variable overrides.
470	// Per OTel conventions, these take highest priority for telemetry settings.
471	func applyTelemetryEnvOverrides(cfg *Config) {
472		if endpoint := os.Getenv("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT"); endpoint != "" {
473			cfg.Telemetry.Endpoint = endpoint
474		}
475	
476		if serviceName := os.Getenv("OTEL_SERVICE_NAME"); serviceName != "" {
477			cfg.Telemetry.ServiceName = serviceName
478		}
479	
480		// Note: OTEL_SDK_DISABLED is handled in IsTelemetryEnabled() rather than
481		// stored in the config struct, since there is no Enabled field — telemetry
482		// is enabled when an endpoint is configured.
483	}
484	
485	// ConfigValidationResult holds the result of validating a config file.
486	type ConfigValidationResult struct {
487		Path        string   // Path to the config file
488		Exists      bool     // Whether the file exists
489		ValidTOML   bool     // Whether the file is valid TOML
490		ParseError  string   // TOML parse error message, if any
491		UnknownKeys []string // Keys in the file that don't map to any known config field
492	}
493	
494	// ValidateConfigFile checks a config file for validity and schema conformance.
495	// Returns a result indicating whether the file exists, is valid TOML, and
496	// whether it contains any unknown sections or properties.
497	func ValidateConfigFile(path string) ConfigValidationResult {
498		result := ConfigValidationResult{Path: path}
499	
500		// Check if file exists
501		if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
502			return result
503		}
504		result.Exists = true
505	
506		// Try to decode and check for unknown keys
507		var cfg Config
508		md, err := toml.DecodeFile(path, &cfg)
509		if err != nil {
510			result.ParseError = err.Error()
511			return result
512		}
513		result.ValidTOML = true
514	
515		// Check for keys in the TOML that didn't map to any struct field.
516		// Filter out child keys when the parent section is already unknown
517		// (e.g., if [bogus] is unknown, don't also report bogus.foo).
518		undecoded := md.Undecoded()
519		unknownSections := make(map[string]bool)
520		for _, key := range undecoded {
521			if len(key) == 1 {
522				unknownSections[key[0]] = true
523			}
524		}
525		for _, key := range undecoded {
526			// Skip child keys whose parent section is already flagged
527			if len(key) > 1 && unknownSections[key[0]] {
528				continue
529			}
530			result.UnknownKeys = append(result.UnknownKeys, key.String())
531		}
532	
533		return result
534	}
535	
536	// applyCLIOverrides applies CLI flag overrides to the config.
537	func applyCLIOverrides(cfg *Config, o *CLIOverrides) {
538		// Local sync
539		if o.OutputDir != "" {
540			cfg.LocalSync.OutputDir = o.OutputDir
541		}
542		if o.LocalTimeZone {
543			enabled := true
544			cfg.LocalSync.LocalTimeZone = &enabled
545		}
546		if o.DebugDir != "" {
547			cfg.Logging.DebugDir = o.DebugDir
548		}
549	
550		// Version check (--no-version-check sets enabled to false)
551		if o.NoVersionCheck {
552			disabled := false
553			cfg.VersionCheck.Enabled = &disabled
554		}
555	
556		// Cloud sync (--no-cloud-sync sets enabled to false)
557		if o.NoCloudSync {
558			disabled := false
559			cfg.CloudSync.Enabled = &disabled
560		}
561	
562		// Local sync (--only-cloud-sync sets enabled to false)
563		if o.OnlyCloudSync {
564			disabled := false
565			cfg.LocalSync.Enabled = &disabled
566		}
567	
568		// Logging flags only override if explicitly set (true)
569		if o.Console {
570			enabled := true
571			cfg.Logging.Console = &enabled
572		}
573		if o.Log {
574			enabled := true
575			cfg.Logging.Log = &enabled
576		}
577		if o.Debug {
578			enabled := true
579			cfg.Logging.Debug = &enabled
580		}
581		if o.Silent {
582			enabled := true
583			cfg.Logging.Silent = &enabled
584		}
585	
586		// Analytics (--no-usage-analytics sets enabled to false)
587		if o.NoAnalytics {
588			disabled := false
589			cfg.Analytics.Enabled = &disabled
590		}
591	
592		// Telemetry
593		if o.TelemetryEndpoint != "" {
594			cfg.Telemetry.Endpoint = o.TelemetryEndpoint
595		}
596		if o.TelemetryServiceName != "" {
597			cfg.Telemetry.ServiceName = o.TelemetryServiceName
598		}
599		if o.NoTelemetryPrompts {
600			disabled := false
601			cfg.Telemetry.Prompts = &disabled
602		}
603	}
604	
605	// --- Getter methods ---
606	
607	// GetOutputDir returns the output directory, or empty string to use default.
608	func (c *Config) GetOutputDir() string {
609		return c.LocalSync.OutputDir
610	}
611	
612	// IsVersionCheckEnabled returns whether version check is enabled.
613	// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
614	func (c *Config) IsVersionCheckEnabled() bool {
615		if c.VersionCheck.Enabled != nil {
616			return *c.VersionCheck.Enabled
617		}
618		return true // default enabled
619	}
620	
621	// IsCloudSyncEnabled returns whether cloud sync is enabled.
622	// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
623	func (c *Config) IsCloudSyncEnabled() bool {
624		if c.CloudSync.Enabled != nil {
625			return *c.CloudSync.Enabled
626		}
627		return true // default enabled
628	}
629	
630	// IsLocalSyncEnabled returns whether local sync is enabled.
631	// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
632	// When false, only cloud sync is performed (equivalent to --only-cloud-sync).
633	func (c *Config) IsLocalSyncEnabled() bool {
634		if c.LocalSync.Enabled != nil {
635			return *c.LocalSync.Enabled
636		}
637		return true // default enabled
638	}
639	
640	// IsConsoleEnabled returns whether console logging is enabled.
641	// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
642	func (c *Config) IsConsoleEnabled() bool {
643		if c.Logging.Console != nil {
644			return *c.Logging.Console
645		}
646		return false // default disabled
647	}
648	
649	// IsLogEnabled returns whether file logging is enabled.
650	// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
651	func (c *Config) IsLogEnabled() bool {
652		if c.Logging.Log != nil {
653			return *c.Logging.Log
654		}
655		return false // default disabled
656	}
657	
658	// IsDebugEnabled returns whether debug logging is enabled.
659	// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
660	func (c *Config) IsDebugEnabled() bool {
661		if c.Logging.Debug != nil {
662			return *c.Logging.Debug
663		}
664		return false // default disabled
665	}
666	
667	// IsSilentEnabled returns whether silent mode is enabled.
668	// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
669	func (c *Config) IsSilentEnabled() bool {
670		if c.Logging.Silent != nil {
671			return *c.Logging.Silent
672		}
673		return false // default disabled
674	}
675	
676	// IsAnalyticsEnabled returns whether analytics are enabled.
677	// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
678	func (c *Config) IsAnalyticsEnabled() bool {
679		if c.Analytics.Enabled != nil {
680			return *c.Analytics.Enabled
681		}
682		return true // default enabled
683	}
684	
685	// IsTelemetryEnabled returns whether telemetry should be enabled.
686	// Telemetry is enabled when an endpoint is configured.
687	// The standard OTEL_SDK_DISABLED env var ("true" or "1") overrides this.
688	func (c *Config) IsTelemetryEnabled() bool {
689		if disabled := os.Getenv("OTEL_SDK_DISABLED"); disabled == "true" || disabled == "1" {
690			return false
691		}
692		return c.Telemetry.Endpoint != ""
693	}
694	
695	// GetTelemetryEndpoint returns the telemetry endpoint, or empty string if not configured.
696	func (c *Config) GetTelemetryEndpoint() string {
697		return c.Telemetry.Endpoint
698	}
699	
700	// GetTelemetryServiceName returns the service name, or empty string to use default.
701	func (c *Config) GetTelemetryServiceName() string {
702		return c.Telemetry.ServiceName
703	}
704	
705	// IsTelemetryPromptsDisabled returns whether prompt text should be excluded from telemetry.
706	// The config field "prompts" defaults to true (included). When set to false, prompts are excluded.
707	func (c *Config) IsTelemetryPromptsDisabled() bool {
708		if c.Telemetry.Prompts != nil {
709			return !*c.Telemetry.Prompts
710		}
711		return false // default: prompts are included
712	}
713	
714	// GetDebugDir returns the custom debug directory, or empty string to use default.
715	func (c *Config) GetDebugDir() string {
716		return c.Logging.DebugDir
717	}
718	
719	// IsLocalTimeZoneEnabled returns whether local timezone is enabled.
720	// Defaults to false if not explicitly set (UTC is default).
721	func (c *Config) IsLocalTimeZoneEnabled() bool {
722		if c.LocalSync.LocalTimeZone != nil {
723			return *c.LocalSync.LocalTimeZone
724		}
725		return false
726	}
727	
728	// GetProviderCmd returns the custom execution command for a provider, or empty
729	// string if none is configured. The providerID should match a registered
730	// provider ID (e.g., "claude", "codex", "cursor", "droid", "gemini").
731	func (c *Config) GetProviderCmd(providerID string) string {
732		switch strings.ToLower(providerID) {
733		case "claude":
734			return c.Providers.ClaudeCmd
735		case "codex":
736			return c.Providers.CodexCmd
737		case "cursor":
738			return c.Providers.CursorCmd
739		case "deepseek":
740			return c.Providers.DeepSeekCmd
741		case "droid":
742			return c.Providers.DroidCmd
743		case "gemini":
744			return c.Providers.GeminiCmd
745		default:
746			return ""
747		}
748	}
```

I've reviewed the PR. Now let me write the line-by-line review.

# PR #218 Code Review — `feat: add DeepSeek TUI provider`

## Overall context

The PR adds a new provider package `pkg/providers/deepseek/` (~3,000 lines including tests) and wires it into the registry + config. The package follows the established `droidcli` provider structure very closely — which is its biggest strength (consistency) and its biggest weakness (large amounts of near-duplicated code).

---

## Line-by-line review

### `pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

**Lines 16, 81–83 (added):** Imports the new package and registers `deepseek` with the ID `"deepseek"`.

- (1) ✅ Registration follows the exact pattern used for every other provider. One-line import + three-line registration block — minimal blast radius.
- (2) ⚠️ The registry already lists 6 providers, each with an identical three-line block. Not in scope for this PR, but a `register(id string, factory func() spi.Provider)` helper would let this entire function shrink to 6 lines. Worth a follow-up issue.

---

### `pkg/config/config.go`

**Lines 116–117 (added):** Adds `# DeepSeek TUI command\n# deepseek_cmd = "deepseek"` to the default template, **alphabetically out of order** with the surrounding commands (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Droid, Gemini are alphabetical; DeepSeek is appended last).

- (3) ⚠️ Cosmetic: place `deepseek_cmd` between `cursor_cmd` and `droid_cmd` so the template stays alphabetized like the existing entries.

**Line 198 (added):** `DeepSeekCmd string \`toml:"deepseek_cmd"\`` in `ProvidersConfig`.

- (4) ✅ Correctly placed alphabetically in the struct (the struct is in sorted order; only the template comment is out of order).

**Lines 739–740 (added):** New case in `GetProviderCmd` switch.

- (5) ✅ Correctly placed alphabetically and uses `strings.ToLower` already at the top of the switch.

---

### `pkg/providers/deepseek/provider.go`

**Lines 18–21 (new):** `defaultCommand = "deepseek"`, `versionFlag = "--version"`.

- (6) ✅ Constants pulled out for clarity. Good.

**Lines 31–33 (new):** `Name()` returns `"DeepSeek TUI"`.

- (7) ✅ Matches the provider's actual product name.

**Lines 37–73 (new):** `Check(customCommand)` — looks up binary, runs `--version`, classifies failures, emits analytics.

- (8) ✅ The flow is identical to `droidcli/provider.go`'s `Check()`. That's appropriate — consistent UX across providers.
- (9) ⚠️ **DRY violation**: `classifyCheckError`, `buildCheckErrorMessage`, `trackCheckSuccess`, `trackCheckFailure` (lines 247–315) are **byte-for-byte identical** to the droidcli versions (verified via `diff`). With each new provider we copy ~70 lines of generic logic. These belong in `pkg/spi` (e.g. `spi.CheckBinary(...)`). Not necessarily a blocker for this PR — the pattern was set before — but worth flagging as growing tech debt.
- (10) ✅ Good observability: structured `slog.Info` at each branch (`"binary resolved"`, `"version probe failed"`, `"succeeded"`). Matches CLAUDE.md guidance.
- (11) ✅ Properly separates `cmdName` (what the user asked for) from `resolved` (what was on PATH) in both analytics and error messages.
- (12) ❓ Line 38 discards args from `parseCommand` with `_`. Intentional — `Check` only probes the binary, not its args. Fine, but a brief `// args ignored: Check probes the binary, not its flags` comment would explain the discard.

**Lines 77–103 (new):** `DetectAgent(projectPath, helpOutput)`.

- (13) ✅ Three early-return guard clauses (error, empty list, empty projectPath) — idiomatic Go.
- (14) ✅ Reuses `sessionMentionsProject` rather than reading/parsing the full JSON. Good for performance on the watcher hot path.
- (15) ⚠️ Multiple exit points (4 `return`s plus the loop `return`). Could use a `found bool` and a single trailing return, but the current shape is clear and readable, so this is borderline acceptable.

**Lines 107–144 (new):** `GetAgentChatSessions` — iterates session files, calls `parseSessionFile`, `convertToAgentSession`, accumulates results.

- (16) ✅ Progress callback is correctly invoked even on the error/skip path.
- (17) ⚠️ `result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(matchingFiles))` uses `len(matchingFiles)`; `totalFiles` already holds that value (line 123). Minor — just use `totalFiles`.
- (18) ✅ Errors per-file logged at `Debug` and skipped — one corrupt file doesn't kill the whole sync. Good.

**Lines 147–160 (new):** `GetAgentChatSession` — single-session variant.

- (19) ⚠️ Line 149: `if err != nil || path == "" { return nil, err }`. If `path == ""` and `err == nil`, this returns `(nil, nil)`. That's intentional ("not found, not an error"), but two callers might trip over it because `nil, nil` is unusual in Go. A comment like `// path == "" with err == nil means "no such session"` would help.
- (20) ✅ Reuses `parseSessionFile` and `convertToAgentSession` — DRY within the package.

**Lines 164–200 (new):** `ExecAgentAndWatch` — spawns the watcher in a goroutine, runs the binary in the foreground, then waits for the watcher to exit.

- (21) ✅ Good: `context.WithCancel` + `defer cancel()` + waiting on `watchErr` channel ensures clean teardown.
- (22) ✅ Uses `errors.Is(werr, context.Canceled)` to suppress the expected cancellation error. Idiomatic.
- (23) ✅ Heavy `slog.Info` instrumentation at every state transition.

**Lines 204–213 (new):** `WatchAgent` — pure watcher path (no exec).

- (24) ✅ Clean delegation to `watchSessions`. Nil-callback guard with a clear error message.

**Lines 216–243 (new):** `ListAgentChatSessions` — lightweight metadata-only listing.

- (25) ✅ Good performance design — calls `extractSessionMetadata` (which uses `parseSessionFile(path, false)` to avoid retaining raw bytes). Comment on line 88–89 explains the `wantRawData=false` choice well.

**Lines 247–260 (new):** `classifyCheckError` — switch on `errors.As`/`errors.Is`.

- (26) ⚠️ **Duplication** (see #9). Identical to droidcli.
- (27) ⚠️ Line 251: `execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound` would be more idiomatic as `errors.Is(execErr, exec.ErrNotFound)` (it survives future wrapping). Minor.

**Lines 262–287 (new):** `buildCheckErrorMessage` — produces user-facing strings.

- (28) ✅ Uses `strings.Builder` correctly with `fmt.Fprintf(&builder, ...)` for safe formatting.
- (29) ⚠️ The user-facing strings (`"DeepSeek TUI was not found."`, install hints, etc.) are the only per-provider content; the rest of this function is structurally identical to droidcli. A `spi.RenderCheckError(providerName, errorType, ...)` helper would let each provider supply just the name and install URL.

**Lines 289–315 (new):** `trackCheckSuccess`/`trackCheckFailure`.

- (30) ⚠️ Byte-for-byte identical to droidcli (verified). Definitely belongs in `pkg/spi` or `pkg/analytics`.

**Lines 317–320 (new):** `printDetectionHelp`.

- (31) ✅ Short, clear, uses `log.UserMessage` (the project's convention) instead of `fmt.Println`. Matches CLAUDE.md.

---

### `pkg/providers/deepseek/agent_session.go`

**Lines 16–24:** Type aliases for shared schema types.

- (32) ✅ Convenient and reduces noise in the rest of the file. Matches the droidcli pattern.

**Lines 27–63:** `dsSession`/`dsMetadata`/`dsMessage`/`dsContentPart` JSON structs.

- (33) ✅ Comments explain each field's purpose, especially the polymorphism in `dsContentPart` (`Text` for `"text"`, `Thinking` for `"thinking"`, etc.).
- (34) ⚠️ Line 32: `RawData string \`json:"-"\`` — using `-` tag on an unexported-style field is correct, but the comment "populated only when parseSessionFile is called with wantRawData=true" lives in two places (here and on `parseSessionFile`). Pick one.
- (35) ⚠️ Lines 53–63: Comment on line 56 says "tool_use fields are flat in the JSON (not nested)" — good "why" comment. But the struct mixes `tool_use` and `tool_result` fields together with the same JSON tag namespace (`"content"` is used for both `Text`-content-like and `ToolResultContent`). Since `cp.Text` and `cp.ToolResultContent` both map to JSON key... wait — they don't. `Text` is `json:"text"` and `ToolResultContent` is `json:"content"`. That's correct, but the dual-purpose `Content` JSON key (assistant text vs. tool result content) is exactly the kind of thing that needs a "why DeepSeek's schema does this" comment.

**Lines 70–85:** `parseSessionFile(path, wantRawData)`.

- (36) ✅ Good doc comment explains the memory tradeoff for the boolean. Single-purpose function.

**Lines 90–113:** `extractSessionMetadata` — lightweight metadata extraction.

- (37) ⚠️ Multiple returns from a function declared with `var result *spi.SessionMetadata` at the top — the early-return idiom would be cleaner. The current pattern (declare, assign in middle, return at end) reads like single-exit, but the early `return nil, err` on line 95 breaks that promise. Either go full single-exit or full guard-clause.

**Lines 117–127:** `firstUserMessageText`.

- (38) ✅ Simple, clear, single exit (the `return ""` after the loop).

**Lines 130–162:** `convertToAgentSession`.

- (39) ✅ Two guard clauses (nil session, no messages), then main logic, then return. Idiomatic.
- (40) ✅ Falls back to `"deepseek-session"` if slug is empty — important for filename safety.
- (41) ⚠️ Line 152: `if debugRaw { writeDebugRaw(session) }` is a side effect mixed into a "convert" function. Caller-side would be cleaner, but this matches the droidcli pattern, so consistency wins.

**Lines 165–202:** `generateAgentSession`.

- (42) ✅ **Excellent comment block lines 182–184** explaining why we *don't* synthesize Usage from `TotalTokens`. Pure "why" — exactly what CLAUDE.md asks for.
- (43) ✅ Line 173: rejects sessions with no exchanges via error rather than producing empty output.

**Lines 208–273:** `buildExchanges`.

- (44) ✅ **Excellent comment lines 212–215** explaining why we use `CreatedAt`/`UpdatedAt` rather than `time.Now()` — deterministic re-parse output. The unit test `TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps` validates exactly this.
- (45) ✅ Logic for distinguishing real user turns from tool-result-only turns (line 223–231) is clear and well-commented.
- (46) ⚠️ Line 224: `if isToolResultOnly(&msg)` — `msg` is the loop variable; passing `&msg` is a footgun in older Go (pre-1.22 captured loop-var bug). Go 1.22+ this is safe, but if you ever hold onto that pointer past the loop iteration (which `isToolResultOnly` doesn't, so it's fine), it'd bite you. The function could take `dsMessage` by value just like `attachToolResults` and `convertUserMessage` do — pass value, no pointer ambiguity.

**Lines 277–292:** `isToolResultOnly`.

- (47) ✅ Well-named, single-purpose, doc comment explains the edge case (`<turn_meta>` filtering).
- (48) ⚠️ Loop assignment style: `hasToolResult := false` then mutate inside the switch then `return hasToolResult` at the bottom. Could return `true` directly when both conditions are met (`hasToolResult && all-text-is-empty-or-turn_meta`), but the current shape is fine.

**Lines 307–342:** `attachToolResults`.

- (49) ✅ **Best comment in the PR** (lines 294–306). Explains the bug it fixes (parallel tool calls misattributed when iterating by position), the design (index by `UseID`), and the rationale. This is exactly what CLAUDE.md asks for.
- (50) ✅ Re-renders `FormattedMarkdown` after attaching results — the two-phase render pattern is intentional and tested.

**Lines 344–370:** `convertUserMessage`/`userContentParts`.

- (51) ✅ Filtering `<turn_meta>` here means downstream renderers don't see system-injected blobs. Comment explains the why on line 346.

**Lines 375–417:** `convertAssistantMessage`.

- (52) ✅ Splits assistant content into "text/thinking bundled together" vs "each tool_use is its own message" — matches the rendered markdown structure.
- (53) ⚠️ Lines 386–388 (and 393–395): fallback `if thinking == "" { thinking = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text) }`. This crosses fields — a `thinking`-typed content part falls back to `cp.Text` if `cp.Thinking` is empty. Is that real DeepSeek behavior (do they sometimes put thinking text in the `text` field)? If yes, a comment explaining "DeepSeek sometimes emits thinking content with the text field populated instead of thinking" would clarify; if no, the fallback is dead defensive code.

**Lines 422–443:** `convertToolUseMessage`.

- (54) ✅ Falls back to `"unknown"` for unnamed tools, with a unit test covering this case.

**Lines 446–483:** `classifyToolType`.

- (55) ⚠️ Five small `map[string]bool` literals constructed on every call. They could be package-level `var` for a (tiny) perf win and clearer that these are static. Not blocking.
- (56) ⚠️ Lots of multiple-return-points (one per category). A single switch with a fall-through to default would be cleaner:
  ```go
  switch name {
  case "write_file", "edit_file", "apply_patch":
      return schema.ToolTypeWrite
  case "read_file", ...:
      return schema.ToolTypeRead
  ...
  default:
      return schema.ToolTypeGeneric
  }
  ```
  Removes the map allocations entirely.

**Lines 487–507:** `msgContentText`.

- (57) ⚠️ Two-pass scan (lines 488–495 then 497–505) feels wasteful but each pass has slightly different intent (visible text vs. any non-empty fallback). A brief comment explaining "two passes: prefer text content, fall back to thinking/anything non-empty" would help.

**Lines 510–520:** `deriveSlug`.

- (58) ✅ Clean. Default slug `"deepseek-session"` is the same value used in the empty-fallback path of `convertToAgentSession` (line 148), so behavior is consistent.

**Lines 523–541:** `writeDebugRaw`.

- (59) ✅ Nil-safe, errors are logged at Debug only. Appropriate for a debug-only side path.

---

### `pkg/providers/deepseek/deepseek_exec.go`

**Lines 15–24:** `expandTilde`.

- (60) ✅ Simple, single-purpose. Falls back to original path if `os.UserHomeDir` errors — safe.

**Lines 28–36:** `parseCommand`.

- (61) ✅ Returns `(defaultCommand, nil)` when empty — single sensible default.

**Lines 39–52:** `ExecuteDeepSeek`.

- (62) ✅ Wires stdin/stdout/stderr to the parent process — correct for a TUI.

**Lines 56–82:** `ensureResumeArgs`.

- (63) ✅ Thorough handling of `--resume <id>`, `-r <id>`, `--resume=<id>`, dangling `--resume`, etc. Validated by 11 test cases.
- (64) ⚠️ Line 66: `repaired := append([]string{}, args[:i+1]...)` — defensive copy to avoid mutating the caller's slice. Good. Same pattern on line 75. Could use `slices.Concat(args[:i+1], []string{resumeSessionID}, args[i+1:])` in modern Go for clarity.
- (65) ❓ Line 63 condition is `strings.TrimSpace(args[i+1]) != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(args[i+1], "-")` — what if a user genuinely wants `--resume some-session-with-dash`? The dash check assumes any dash-prefixed token is a flag. This is reasonable but worth a comment: `// next token starting with - is treated as a flag, not a session id`.

---

### `pkg/providers/deepseek/markdown_tools.go`

**Lines 20–35:** `formatToolCall`.

- (66) ✅ Clear two-phase render contract documented in the comment (called once with input only, then again after the result is attached).

**Lines 37–68:** `formatToolInput` — big switch.

- (67) ✅ Falls back to `renderGenericJSON` for unknown tools — good degraded behavior.
- (68) ⚠️ Lines 63–67: `case "taskcreate", "taskread", "tasklist", "note":` returns `renderGenericJSON(args)`, and the `default` also returns `renderGenericJSON(args)`. The explicit case is dead code — remove it or add a `// these are intentionally rendered as raw JSON for now` comment.

**Lines 70–83:** `formatToolOutput`.

- (69) ✅ Multi-line vs single-line branching is reasonable; matches the rendering choice in droidcli.

**Lines 89–135:** `extractPathHints`.

- (70) ✅ Comment lines 86–88 explicitly references the droidcli mirror — good cross-reference for future maintainers.
- (71) ⚠️ This function is nearly identical in structure to droidcli's. A `spi.ExtractPathHintsFromInput(input, workspaceRoot, fieldNames []string)` helper could host the body while each provider supplies its own field list — but again, that's a refactor outside this PR's scope.

**Lines 137–149:** `addPathHint`.

- (72) ✅ De-dups linearly — fine for tiny hint slices. Tested.

**Lines 153–172:** `renderReadInput`.

- (73) ✅ Falls back to generic JSON if no recognized field.
- (74) ⚠️ Lines 162–170: the `if offset != "" || limit != "" { ... }` block fills in `"0"` / `"?"` defaults. Reasonable, but the `"?"` is unusual rendering — worth a comment that "limit unspecified" was a real DeepSeek case.

**Lines 199–204:** `renderFileSearchInput`.

- (75) ✅ Simple guard, clean fallback.

**Lines 248–267:** `renderEditInput`.

- (76) ⚠️ Many possible `stringValue` keys for the same logical concept (`old_str|old_text|old_string|old`). Either DeepSeek emits all of these (in which case a comment "DeepSeek tools use varying field names across versions" would help), or this is defensive over-engineering. If the schema is stable, drop the synonyms.

**Lines 277–303:** `renderTodoWrite`.

- (77) ⚠️ Line 286 inside the fallback loop reuses `ok` shadowing the outer scope — minor but worth a different name (`hasItems`).
- (78) ⚠️ Line 294: `item, _ := raw.(map[string]any)` — if the assertion fails, `item` is nil and `stringValue(nil, ...)` then returns `""`. Works correctly but the intent is implicit. A small `if item == nil { continue }` would be clearer.

**Lines 321–342:** `formatDiffBlock`.

- (79) 🐛 **Awkward construction**: the `if oldText == "" { break }` check sits *inside* the loop. `strings.Split("", "\n")` returns `[""]`, so the loop runs once with a useless empty line before breaking. Cleaner:
  ```go
  if oldText != "" {
      for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
          b.WriteString("-")
          b.WriteString(line)
          b.WriteString("\n")
      }
  }
  ```
  Same for `newText`. Functionally equivalent today but the current code's intent isn't obvious to a reader.

**Lines 344–369:** `formatContentBlock`/`languageFromPath`.

- (80) ✅ Backtick escaping (`escaped := strings.ReplaceAll(content, "```", "\\```")`) prevents fence-injection in markdown — important for safety.
- (81) ⚠️ `languageFromPath` only special-cases `yml→yaml` and `md→markdown`. Many other ext→lang mappings exist (`ts→typescript`, etc.). Probably fine but worth knowing — a follow-up that shares a single language-resolution helper across providers would be ideal.

**Lines 373–411:** `renderGenericJSON`/`renderJSONValue`.

- (82) ⚠️ Near-identical to droidcli (only variable names differ — `keys` vs `ordered`, `b` vs `builder`, `i,k` vs `idx,key`). Strong candidate for shared helper.

**Lines 413–435:** `stringValue`.

- (83) ⚠️ Droidcli's version supports more types (`fmt.Stringer`, `[]byte`, `float32`, `int32`, `uint`, `uint64`). DeepSeek's is a subset. If a tool ever passes a `float32`, DeepSeek's `stringValue` will silently return `""`. Either match droidcli's coverage or add a comment explaining why the smaller set is sufficient.

**Lines 437–443:** `normalizeToolName`.

- (84) ✅ Lowercase + strip separators. Simple and tested implicitly via `TestFormatToolInput`.

**Lines 445–454:** `todoSymbol`.

- (85) ✅ Compact mapping with sensible default.

---

### `pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go`

**Lines 14–17:** Constants.

- (86) ✅ Named constants for `.deepseek` and `sessions` — better than magic strings.

**Lines 36–77:** `listSessionFiles`.

- (87) ✅ Returns empty slice (not error) when directory doesn't exist — correct behavior, prevents detection from failing on first run.
- (88) ⚠️ Line 60: `info, err := entry.Info()` errors are silently swallowed (`continue`). Should at least `slog.Debug` so a permission issue is visible.

**Lines 81–94:** `findSessionFileByID`.

- (89) 🐛 **Performance**: lists every file in the directory then linearly scans. Since DeepSeek's IDs match filenames (`<uuid>.json`), this could be one `os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, sessionID+".json"))`. With many sessions this matters.
- (90) ⚠️ Line 89: `candidate == sessionID || file.FileName == sessionID` — the second branch covers the unusual case where the caller passes a filename with `.json` suffix. Worth a comment.

**Lines 96–102:** `trimExt`.

- (91) ✅ Tiny helper, clear. Could just be `strings.TrimSuffix(name, filepath.Ext(name))` inline at the one call site — but extracted is fine for testability.

**Lines 106–123:** `sessionMentionsProject`.

- (92) ✅ Calls `extractWorkspaceFast` first (cheap), then canonicalizes both sides. Sensible order.

**Lines 129–157:** `extractWorkspaceFast`.

- (93) ✅ **Excellent why-comment** (lines 125–128 and 137–140) explaining the streaming approach and the 1MB buffer raise. Both are answered by unit tests (`TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine`, `TestExtractWorkspaceFast`).
- (94) ⚠️ This is custom JSON parsing that assumes specific formatting (workspace appears textually before `"messages"`). If DeepSeek ever reorders metadata keys, this breaks silently. Worth either a comment "DeepSeek writes metadata before messages — this assumption is verified by the tests" or a fallback to full JSON parse.

**Lines 160–180:** `matchWorkspace`.

- (95) ⚠️ Hand-rolled JSON value extraction with quote tracking. Does not handle escaped quotes in the value (e.g. workspace path containing `\"`). Probably impossible in practice (workspace is a real filesystem path) but a comment "assumes no escaped quotes in workspace value" would be honest.

**Lines 183–196:** `canonicalizePath`.

- (96) ✅ Three-tier fallback (canonical → abs → clean) — robust. Single exit per branch, but the fallthroughs are equivalent to guard clauses.

---

### `pkg/providers/deepseek/watcher.go`

**Lines 15–17:** `watchState` struct.

- (97) ⚠️ One field — could be inlined as `map[string]int64`. Wrapping in a struct anticipates future state, which violates "Don't design for hypothetical future requirements" from CLAUDE.md. That said, droidcli uses the same shape for consistency.

**Lines 21–68:** `watchSessions`.

- (98) ✅ Standard fsnotify event loop with proper cleanup. Initial scan happens before subscribing — slight race window where a file created between scan and watch could be missed, but unlikely on a sessions dir that doesn't churn.
- (99) ⚠️ Line 42–44: initial scan failure is debug-logged only and execution continues. Probably right (a single corrupt file shouldn't kill the watcher), but a `slog.Warn` for "initial scan failed" might surface real misconfigurations sooner.

**Lines 73–88:** `setupWatcher`.

- (100) ✅ Walks up from `~/.deepseek/sessions` → `~/.deepseek` → `~` as parents go missing. Clever and correct.

**Lines 91–127:** `handleWatchEvent`.

- (101) ✅ Recursively re-sets up the watcher when `sessions/` is freshly created — handles the cold-start case.
- (102) ✅ Filters to `.json` files only.

**Lines 130–158:** `processSessionFile`.

- (103) ✅ `lastProcessed` map ensures the same modtime isn't reprocessed (events can fire multiple times).
- (104) ⚠️ Line 137: `last >= modTime` — equal `modTime` skips. On fast successive writes within the same nanosecond, the second write is dropped. Acceptable for session files (humans don't type that fast), but worth a comment.

**Lines 161–187:** `scanAndProcessSessions`.

- (105) ⚠️ Bulk of this is duplicated in `processSessionFile`. Could extract a `processIfChanged(file sessionFile, ...) bool` to share the modtime-check + parse + dispatch logic.

**Lines 190–202:** `dispatchSession`.

- (106) ✅ Goroutine + panic recovery — sensible for callbacks that might panic on bad data. Matches droidcli.
- (107) ⚠️ Spawning unbounded goroutines: if the initial scan finds 1,000 sessions, you get 1,000 concurrent callbacks. Should probably be a bounded worker pool — but droidcli does the same thing, so consistency wins for this PR.

---

## Test review

### `agent_session_test.go` (522 lines)

- (108) ✅ `TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps` (lines 14–42) — **excellent** test. Tests real logic (the deterministic-timestamp fix), would catch a regression to `time.Now()`, has a clear comment explaining what it's protecting.
- (109) ✅ `TestBuildExchanges_FallbackToCreatedAt` (lines 45–65) — good coverage of the empty-UpdatedAt case.
- (110) ✅ `TestAttachToolResults_ParallelToolCalls` (lines 71–134) — **best test in the PR**. Explicitly inverts result order, then asserts each result lands on its correct tool by ID, *plus* asserts the negative (A doesn't get B's result). The comment on lines 130–133 calls out "the old bug" — outstanding regression coverage.
- (111) ✅ `TestAttachToolResults_OrphanResultDropped` and `_EmptyContentSkipped` — small, targeted, well-named.
- (112) ✅ `TestIsToolResultOnly` (lines 170–225) — proper data-driven test, 6 cases covering the truth table including the `<turn_meta>` edge case.
- (113) ✅ `TestConvertAssistantMessage` (lines 227–314) — data-driven with per-case assertion functions. A bit heavy on the assertion functions (vs. comparing expected slices), but the assertions are specific and meaningful.
- (114) ✅ `TestGenerateAgentSession_MetadataWorkspaceAndUsage` (lines 316–386) — covers the workspace-override logic AND verifies the deliberate-no-Usage decision documented in agent_session.go:182. Strong test.
- (115) ✅ `TestConvertAssistantMessage_AttachesPathHints` (lines 388–410) — covers the shell-command path extraction path.
- (116) ✅ `TestClassifyToolType` — data-driven, but only 8 cases (the function has ~25 mapped names). Worth expanding to cover at least one name per category and at least one alias (e.g. `task_shell_start`).
- (117) ✅ `TestDeriveSlug` — covers the four meaningful cases (real text, skip turn_meta, empty, no user msg).
- (118) ✅ `TestBuildExchanges_TwoTurns` — covers exchange boundary detection.

### `deepseek_exec_test.go` (182 lines)

- (119) ✅ `TestEnsureResumeArgs` — fully data-driven with 11 cases. Excellent.
- (120) ✅ `TestParseCommand` — 6 cases including the home-expansion case.
- (121) ✅ `TestParseCommand_DoesNotMutate` (lines 164–182) — guards against accidental input mutation and slice aliasing. Defensive but not tautological — would catch a real bug.

### `markdown_tools_test.go` (348 lines)

- (122) ✅ `TestFormatToolCall_ReadFile_InputThenOutput` — tests the two-phase render contract directly. Important.
- (123) ✅ `TestFormatToolOutput` — data-driven, 6 cases covering nil/empty/whitespace/single-line/multi-line/non-string.
- (124) ✅ `TestFormatToolInput` (lines 100–246) — data-driven, 11 tool types covered. Could add a "field synonym" case (e.g. `old_str` vs `old_text`) to validate the synonym list in `renderEditInput` actually works.
- (125) ✅ `TestRenderGenericJSON_SortsKeys` — small but catches a real determinism bug.
- (126) ✅ `TestExtractPathHints` — data-driven, includes de-dup and array-walking cases.

### `path_utils_test.go` (209 lines)

- (127) ✅ `TestExtractWorkspaceFast` (lines 17–87) — table-driven with 6 cases including the critical "workspace-key-in-tool-result is ignored" case. Has an excellent block comment.
- (128) ✅ `TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine` — proves the 1MB scanner buffer raise actually works. Constructs a 200KB line.
- (129) ✅ `TestExtractWorkspaceFast_MissingFile` — tiny but verifies the no-error behavior.
- (130) ✅ `TestSessionMentionsProject` — data-driven with a factory pattern for content/projectDir per case.
- (131) ✅ `TestCanonicalizePath` — wisely uses `t.TempDir()` instead of asserting specific strings (avoids `/var` vs `/private/var` on macOS). Three sub-cases covering meaningful behaviors.

### `provider_test.go` (135 lines)

- (132) 🐛 `TestClassifyCheckError` (lines 10–46): the test case named **`"nil error returns version_failed"`** actually passes `errors.New("boom")`, not nil. The label is misleading. Either fix the label to `"opaque error returns version_failed"` or actually pass `nil` (which would currently classify as `"version_failed"` since none of the `errors.As`/`errors.Is` checks would match — but you'd want to confirm that's the intended behavior).
- (133) ✅ `TestBuildCheckErrorMessage` — data-driven, 5 cases, each asserting required substrings (not exact strings, so message wording can evolve without breaking tests).
- (134) ✅ `TestDetectAgent_NoSessionsReturnsFalse` and `_EmptyProjectMatchesAnySession` — use `t.Setenv("HOME", tmp)` cleanly to sandbox. Both test meaningful semantics.

### What's NOT tested

- (135) ⚠️ `extractPathHints` doesn't test the `cwd` resolution chain in shell commands (lines 122–128 of markdown_tools.go) — the cwd-fallback logic could regress silently.
- (136) ⚠️ `ExecuteDeepSeek` and the `WatchAgent`/`ExecAgentAndWatch` integration are not tested. Hard to test without process spawning, so acceptable.
- (137) ⚠️ `watchSessions` event-loop behavior (`Create` vs `Write` filtering, modtime dedup) has no test. fsnotify is fiddly to test, but a test with `t.TempDir()` + manual writes would catch deduplication regressions.

---

## Top 5 most important improvements

1. **Fix the misleading test name in `provider_test.go:17`** — `"nil error returns version_failed"` does not actually pass `nil`. Either rename the case or add an actual nil-error test (which would surface that the function doesn't explicitly handle nil today). **(observation #132)**

2. **Clean up `formatDiffBlock`** (markdown_tools.go:321–342) — replace the in-loop `if oldText == "" { break }` with an outer `if oldText != "" { for ... }` guard. Same for `newText`. The current code is functionally correct but reads awkwardly. **(observation #79)**

3. **Optimize `findSessionFileByID`** (path_utils.go:81–94) — change from "list all files, linear scan" to `os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, sessionID+".json"))`. Since the contract is that filenames are `<uuid>.json`, this is O(1) vs O(N). **(observation #89)**

4. **Open a follow-up issue for cross-provider DRY** — `classifyCheckError`, `buildCheckErrorMessage`, `trackCheckSuccess`, `trackCheckFailure`, `renderGenericJSON`, `renderJSONValue`, `stringValue`, `normalizeToolName`, `todoSymbol`, `formatContentBlock`, `languageFromPath`, `formatDiffBlock`, and the entire watcher.go event loop are now duplicated across multiple providers. Each new provider compounds this. A `pkg/spi/providerhelpers` (or similar) package could host the generic logic. Out of scope for this PR, but worth tracking before adding provider #7. **(observations #9, #29, #30, #71, #82, #105)**

5. **Add a "field synonym" test for `renderEditInput`** (markdown_tools.go:248–267) — the function accepts `old_str|old_text|old_string|old` and `new_str|new_text|new_string|new`. If those synonyms exist because DeepSeek really emits all four forms, a test should cover each. If they don't, drop the synonyms and simplify. **(observation #76)**

---

## Unit-test recommendations

Tests already cover the genuinely complicated logic (parallel tool-result attribution, deterministic timestamp generation, fast workspace extraction, scanner buffer raise). The new tests I'd add:

- **`extractWorkspaceFast` with escaped quotes in workspace value** — confirm matchWorkspace's hand-rolled parser handles (or explicitly doesn't handle) `"/path/with\"quote"`. The current code would terminate at the first `"`, but it's worth pinning the contract.
- **`extractPathHints` with shell command using `workdir` vs `cwd` vs neither** — exercises the cwd resolution chain in markdown_tools.go:122–128 that's currently untested.
- **`renderEditInput` synonym coverage** — see top-5 item #5 above.

Tests I would NOT add:

- More cases in `classifyToolType` — the mapping is data, not logic. The current 8 cases are sufficient as a smoke test.
- Tests for `ExecuteDeepSeek` — wraps `exec.Cmd.Run()`, tests would be testing the stdlib.
- Tests for fsnotify event delivery — testing the library, not our code.

Overall: this is a solid, well-structured provider implementation with strong tests. The main improvements are minor cleanups and the broader architectural observation about cross-provider DRY.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-18 13:49:22Z)**_

Verified the end-to-end wiring. The chain is intact:

1. **CLI dispatch** — `main.go:348` sets `providerID = "deepseek"` from `specstory run deepseek`.
2. **Config lookup** — `main.go:371` calls `loadedConfig.GetProviderCmd("deepseek")` when no `-c` flag was passed.
3. **Config resolution** — `config.go:739-740` returns `c.Providers.DeepSeekCmd` (the value loaded from the `deepseek_cmd` TOML key on line 198).
4. **Pass to provider** — `main.go:476` passes `customCmd` as the second arg to `provider.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, customCmd, ...)`.
5. **Provider honors it** — `deepseek/provider.go:174` and `:187` both forward `customCommand` to `ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)`.
6. **Final use** — `deepseek/deepseek_exec.go:40` calls `parseCommand(customCommand)`, which splits the configured command (with tilde expansion) and uses `"deepseek"` only as a fallback when the config value is empty.

Case handling is also correct: `GetProviderCmd` lowercases the providerID internally (config.go:732), and the registry lookup is case-insensitive (`strings.EqualFold`), so `specstory run DeepSeek` would still resolve `deepseek_cmd`.

No code changes needed — the config option is already respected. Only the README needed the new row, which is now in place.

Done. `findSessionFileByID` is now O(1):

- **Old**: `listSessionFiles` (read entire dir + `os.Stat` per entry + sort) → linear scan.
- **New**: build `<dir>/<uuid>.json` and a single `os.Stat`.

- Why we stat directly instead of listing
- That callers may pass bare UUID or full filename
- That `("", nil)` means "not found, not an error"

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-18 20:16:07Z)**_

Two fixes:

- **#77 (shadowed `ok`)**: the outer `ok` is actually redundant — when the type assertion fails `itemsRaw` is `nil` and `len(nil) == 0`, so the `!ok ||` is dead. Dropping the outer `ok` eliminates the shadow entirely (cleaner than renaming, since the inner `v, ok :=` is idiomatic Go).
- **#78 (implicit nil)**: add an explicit `if item == nil { continue }`.

```text
1	package deepseektui
2	
3	import (
4		"log/slog"
5		"os"
6		"os/exec"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
11	)
12	
13	// expandTilde expands a leading ~ to the user's home directory so users can
14	// configure custom commands like "~/bin/deepseek".
15	func expandTilde(path string) string {
16		if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
17			return path
18		}
19		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
20		if err != nil {
21			return path
22		}
23		return filepath.Join(home, path[2:])
24	}
25	
26	// parseCommand splits a custom command string into executable name and args.
27	// Returns default command if customCommand is empty.
28	func parseCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {
29		if strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != "" {
30			parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
31			if len(parts) > 0 {
32				return expandTilde(parts[0]), parts[1:]
33			}
34		}
35		return defaultCommand, nil
36	}
37	
38	// ExecuteDeepSeek launches the DeepSeek TUI process and blocks until it exits.
39	func ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand string, resumeSessionID string) error {
40		cmdName, args := parseCommand(customCommand)
41		args = ensureResumeArgs(args, resumeSessionID)
42		if resumeSessionID != "" {
43			slog.Info("ExecuteDeepSeek: resuming session", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
44		}
45	
46		command := exec.Command(cmdName, args...)
47		command.Stdin = os.Stdin
48		command.Stdout = os.Stdout
49		command.Stderr = os.Stderr
50	
51		return command.Run()
52	}
53	
54	// ensureResumeArgs adds --resume <sessionID> to args if not already present.
55	// DeepSeek TUI uses --resume to continue an existing session.
56	func ensureResumeArgs(args []string, resumeSessionID string) []string {
57		if resumeSessionID == "" {
58			return args
59		}
60	
61		for i, arg := range args {
62			if arg == "--resume" || arg == "-r" {
63				if i+1 < len(args) && strings.TrimSpace(args[i+1]) != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(args[i+1], "-") {
64					return args
65				}
66				repaired := append([]string{}, args[:i+1]...)
67				repaired = append(repaired, resumeSessionID)
68				repaired = append(repaired, args[i+1:]...)
69				return repaired
70			}
71			if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--resume=") {
72				if strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(arg, "--resume=")) != "" {
73					return args
74				}
75				repaired := append([]string{}, args...)
76				repaired[i] = "--resume=" + resumeSessionID
77				return repaired
78			}
79		}
80	
81		return append(args, "--resume", resumeSessionID)
82	}
83
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-18 20:17:35Z)**_

All 11 `TestEnsureResumeArgs` cases pass with the modernized implementation. 

The change:
- `slices.Concat(args[:i+1], []string{resumeSessionID}, args[i+1:])` for the insert path — always allocates a new backing array, so the defensive-copy intent is preserved by `slices.Concat`'s contract rather than the manual `append([]string{}, ...)` idiom. One-liner + a brief comment about the allocation guarantee.
- `slices.Clone(args)` for the replace path — clearer than `append([]string{}, args...)`, same semantics.
